description:
Universal-check propagation across the livespec sibling fleet + mechanical wiring-completeness enforcement.

## Problem

SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared code sync — livespec-dev-tooling" (line 487) commits livespec-dev-tooling to ship only universal-by-intent checks: "Enforcement-suite checks that ship in livespec-dev-tooling MUST be those whose intent and CLI surface are stable across every livespec-governed project." But today, wiring those checks into each consumer's `just check` is manual, opt-in, and silently lossy. Audit (2026-05-27):

- `livespec` itself: wires 25 of ~30 canonical checks; missing `check-no-stale-revise-branches` entirely (not even defined in its justfile)
- `livespec-impl-plaintext`: wires 0 of 30 (4 tool-backed only)
- `livespec-dev-tooling` (self-host): wires 0 of 30 (3 tool-backed only)
- `livespec-runtime`: wires 0 of 30 (3 tool-backed only)

The `no_stale_revise_branches.py` check exists in livespec-dev-tooling but runs in zero consumers — it's a shipped check that gates nothing. That's the root-cause failure mode in microcosm: a check exists and no mechanism notices it should be enforced. Same class of failure as the recently-discovered orphaned worktree-template-v2-with-diagrams branch (work shipped to a side-branch, no enforcement of inventory completeness).

## Acceptance

- Every check shipped by livespec-dev-tooling runs in every livespec-governed consumer's `just check` aggregate.
- A new check added to livespec-dev-tooling mechanically forces wiring in every consumer on the next bump-pin PR (in-repo aggregate-completeness check goes red until wired).
- A future sibling generated from `livespec/templates/impl-plugin/` ships with the full canonical aggregate from inception (no "starter scaffold" placeholder state).
- Doctor cross-repo invariant in livespec backstops any adversarial-edit drift.

## Three-layer forever-forward enforcement

1. **In-repo gate** (`aggregate_completeness` check in livespec-dev-tooling): runs in every consumer's `just check`; fires fail on any canonical-check slug missing from the consumer's aggregate or out of canonical order.
2. **Template gate** (`livespec/templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja`): future siblings inherit the full aggregate at copier-copy time.
3. **Cross-repo backstop** (doctor invariant in livespec): walks each registered sibling's local_clone (or GitHub-queries) and asserts every justfile's aggregate matches canonical.

## Phases

- Phase 1 (livespec-dev-tooling): substrate. Audit checks for graceful absence-handling (li-chkabs); add canonical-checks enumeration (li-canon); add aggregate-completeness check (li-aggchk); release v0.3.0 (li-ldtv03).
- Phase 2 (livespec): anchor. Spec change (li-specwr); template update (li-jnjtpl); doctor cross-repo invariant (li-dctxr); self-wire (li-livwir).
- Phase 3 (per-sibling, parallel after 1+2): livespec-impl-plaintext wire (li-ipxwir); livespec-dev-tooling self-host wire (li-ldtwir); livespec-runtime wire (li-runwir).

## Design calls (settled 2026-05-27)

- Canonical-checks enumeration: **dynamic discovery from `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/*.py`** (excluding `_*` helpers + `__init__.py`). Single source of truth = filesystem; hardcoded list would recreate the same drift class this epic eliminates. See updated feedback memory `feedback_static_enumeration_over_dynamic_discovery` for the carve-out rationale (typed dispatch = static; plain-string lists = dynamic).
- Aggregate-completeness gate: **enforces both set-membership AND canonical order** (alphabetical by slug; mechanical, no judgment calls). Out-of-order is also a fail.
- Spec home: **inline in `contracts.md` §"Shared code sync — livespec-dev-tooling"** (it's a property of the existing partition, not a new partition).
title: Universal-check propagation across livespec fleet + wiring-completeness enforcement

description:
Phase 1.1 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

## Scope

Audit each `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/*.py` module for graceful absence-handling and fix any that fail when their precondition is missing. The bar: every check must exit 0 with no findings when invoked in a consumer repo that lacks the check's precondition. This is what makes them safe to wire universally.

Known checks to audit (not exhaustive):

- `vendor_manifest` — exit 0 when no `.vendor.jsonc` exists
- `wrapper_shape` — exit 0 when no `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/` directory exists
- `heading_coverage` — exit 0 when no `tests/heading-coverage.json` exists
- `no_stale_revise_branches` — already correct (enumerates `refs/heads/spec/*` first, exits 0 if empty) — use as the reference pattern
- `claude_md_coverage` — exit 0 when no `CLAUDE.md` exists
- `branch_protection_alignment` — exit 0 when no `branch-protection.json` exists
- `master_ci_green` — exit 0 when no CI configured for the repo

All AST/structural checks (rop_pipeline_shape, no_raise_outside_io, supervisor_discipline, public_api_result_typed, newtype_domain_primitives, no_inheritance, no_write_direct, private_calls, global_writes, keyword_only_args, match_keyword_only, all_declared, main_guard, assert_never_exhaustiveness, pbt_coverage_pure_modules, comment_line_anchors) should already no-op cleanly on empty source trees; verify and document where they don't.

## Acceptance

- Each check has a unit test asserting it exits 0 cleanly when its precondition is absent from a tmp_path scratch directory.
- The audit findings are recorded as a short table in `livespec-dev-tooling`'s changelog for v0.3.0.

## Reference pattern

The existing `no_stale_revise_branches.py` enumerates first and exits 0 on empty list. Apply this shape uniformly.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: audit checks for graceful absence-handling

description:
Phase 1.2 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

## Scope

Add a canonical-checks enumeration surface to livespec-dev-tooling. The list is the single source of truth that drives:

1. The `aggregate_completeness` check (Phase 1.3, li-aggchk) for set-membership + ordering comparison against consumer justfiles.
2. The `livespec/templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` template (Phase 2.2, li-jnjtpl) for stamping the aggregate at copier-copy time.
3. The doctor cross-repo invariant (Phase 2.3, li-dctxr) for fleet-wide drift detection.

## Implementation

- New module: `livespec_dev_tooling/canonical_checks.py`
- Implementation: dynamic discovery via `pkgutil.iter_modules` over `livespec_dev_tooling.checks` (or `importlib.resources.files` walk). Exclude modules whose name starts with `_` (helper convention; `_red_green_replay_modes` already follows this) and `__init__.py`. Map module name (snake_case) to slug (kebab-case): e.g. `keyword_only_args` → `check-keyword-only-args`.
- Public API:
  - `canonical_check_slugs() -> tuple[str, ...]` returning the alphabetically-sorted slug tuple.
  - `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` thin-transport surface emitting `{"slugs": ["check-all-declared", ...]}`.
- Tests covering: alphabetical ordering, underscore-prefix exclusion, __init__.py exclusion, snake_case → kebab-case mapping correctness.

## Design choice

Dynamic discovery from the filesystem (not a hardcoded list) per the 2026-05-27 design call (settled in epic li-univck description). Rationale: hardcoded enumeration would create a second source of truth that silently drifts from the `checks/` directory contents. See feedback memory `feedback_static_enumeration_over_dynamic_discovery` for the typed-dispatch-vs-plain-string carve-out.

## Acceptance

- `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` returns the current set, alphabetically sorted.
- Adding a new `checks/foo.py` file automatically extends the canonical set on next invocation; no other edits required.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: add canonical-checks enumeration (dynamic discovery)

description:
Phase 1.3 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

## Scope

Add new check `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/aggregate_completeness.py` that enforces every consumer's `just check` aggregate contains every canonical check slug, in canonical (alphabetical) order. This is the in-repo gate of the three-layer forever-forward enforcement.

## Algorithm

1. Resolve the consumer's `justfile` location (cwd-relative).
2. Parse the `check:` recipe body; extract the `targets=(...)` array.
3. Filter target list to slugs starting with `check-` (drops blank lines, comments).
4. Read canonical slug tuple via `from livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks import canonical_check_slugs`.
5. Compare:
   - Missing slugs (canonical ∖ wired) → fail with structured finding per missing slug.
   - Out-of-order slugs (set match but ordering differs) → fail with diff between canonical and observed order.
   - Extra slugs (wired ∖ canonical) → NOT a failure (consumers MAY add repo-private checks; they belong at the end after canonical ones).
6. Exit 0 when canonical subset is fully present and in order; exit 4 with structured findings otherwise.

## Critical: this check is itself canonical

Because `aggregate_completeness` lives in `checks/` and is dynamically discovered by `canonical_checks` (Phase 1.2, li-canon), it appears in its own canonical set. That induction means: every consumer is forced to wire `check-aggregate-completeness` into its `just check`, which then enforces wiring of all the other canonical checks. The gate is self-bootstrapping.

## Output discipline

Structlog JSON to stderr; no print, no sys.stderr.write. Follows the same vendored-structlog import pattern as the other checks (sys.path injection at module import time).

## Acceptance

- Unit tests covering: full match (exit 0), missing slug (exit 4 + finding), out-of-order (exit 4 + diff), repo-private extras at the end (exit 0).
- Integration test: when added to a consumer's justfile aggregate, behaves correctly across livespec, impl-plaintext, dev-tooling self-host, runtime.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: add aggregate-completeness check (in-repo gate)

description:
Phase 1.4 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

## Scope

Cut `livespec-dev-tooling v0.3.0` carrying:

- The absence-handling audit fixes from li-chkabs.
- The `livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks` module + thin-transport from li-canon.
- The `aggregate_completeness` check from li-aggchk.
- Self-host wiring of `aggregate_completeness` in livespec-dev-tooling's own `just check` (so it dogfoods from day one).

## Release mechanics

- Follows livespec-dev-tooling's existing release-please workflow (Conventional Commits → semver).
- v0.3.0 is a MINOR bump: new canonical check added (`check-aggregate-completeness`), new public API (`canonical_check_slugs`), no breaking changes to existing checks.
- Tag annotated; release notes summarize the canonical-checks surface and the new gate.

## Acceptance

- `v0.3.0` tag pushed to `github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling`.
- Release notes link back to this epic (li-univck) and the spec change (li-specwr).
- Bump-pin PRs land in li-livwir, li-ipxwir, li-ldtwir, li-runwir against this tag.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: cut v0.3.0 release

description:
Phase 2.1 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec.

## Scope

Extend `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` §"Shared code sync — livespec-dev-tooling" (currently at lines 476–491) with the wiring-completeness invariant. Add prose codifying:

1. **The invariant.** Every check in `livespec-dev-tooling`'s canonical set (dynamically derived from `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/*.py`, excluding `_*` helpers + `__init__.py`) MUST appear in every consumer's `just check` aggregate, in alphabetical order. Consumer-private checks MAY appear after the canonical set.
2. **The in-repo gate.** Every consumer MUST wire `check-aggregate-completeness` (the livespec-dev-tooling check that enforces (1) locally). This is non-optional and self-bootstrapping (the gate enforces its own wiring).
3. **The template gate.** `livespec/templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` MUST stamp the full canonical aggregate at copier-copy time so future siblings inherit the discipline from inception.
4. **The cross-repo backstop.** A new doctor invariant `wiring-completeness-cross-repo` MUST walk each registered sibling's local_clone (or GitHub-query its justfile) and fire fail on any aggregate lacking any canonical slug. This catches adversarial drift the in-repo gate cannot.

## Mechanics

- `/livespec:propose-change` filing a new proposed_change under `SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/wiring-completeness-invariant.md`.
- `/livespec:revise` accepting the change in the next revision pass, producing `SPECIFICATION/history/vNNN/`.
- Update `tests/heading-coverage.json` if any new `## ` heading is added (likely yes, given the three-layer structure).

## Acceptance

- The proposed_change file describes the four-point invariant and the three-layer enforcement structure.
- After revise lands, contracts.md §"Shared code sync — livespec-dev-tooling" carries the new prose inline (per design call: "inline" not new subsection).
- heading-coverage.json updated in lockstep.
title: livespec: propose+revise contracts.md wiring-completeness invariant

description:
Phase 2.2 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec.

## Scope

Update `livespec/templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` so any new sibling generated via `copier copy` ships with the full canonical aggregate from inception, not the current 4-target starter scaffold.

## Implementation

Two design options:

- **Option A (recommended, deferred to implementation):** Static jinja with the canonical aggregate hardcoded into the template. Trade-off: must re-sync the template when livespec-dev-tooling adds a new canonical check. Mitigation: a CI gate in livespec that diffs the template's aggregate against the live `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` output and fails when they drift.
- **Option B:** Generate the aggregate dynamically at copier-copy time by invoking `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` from a copier task hook. Trade-off: makes copier-copy depend on a live livespec-dev-tooling install at the copier-pin tag.

Decision deferred to the implementation PR; both are viable. Document choice in the PR description.

## Acceptance

- New sibling generated from the updated template ships with: all canonical check recipes; aggregate containing all canonical slugs in alphabetical order; `check-aggregate-completeness` in the aggregate.
- A green `just check` is the default state of a freshly-copier-copied sibling (modulo expected Red→Green cleanup of any non-conforming code the new sibling brings).
title: livespec: update templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja with canonical aggregate

description:
Phase 2.3 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec.

## Scope

Add a new doctor static invariant `wiring-completeness-cross-repo` that walks each registered sibling repo and asserts its `just check` aggregate contains every canonical check slug. This is the cross-repo backstop layer of the three-layer enforcement.

## Algorithm

1. Read `.livespec.jsonc` §`cross_repo_targets` to enumerate registered siblings.
2. For each sibling:
   - If `local_clone` configured and the path exists: read `<local_clone>/justfile` directly.
   - Otherwise: GitHub-query the sibling's master-branch `justfile` via `livespec_runtime.cross_repo`.
3. Parse the `check:` aggregate; extract slugs.
4. Fetch canonical slug set: `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` (against the pinned tag).
5. Compare set-membership AND order.
6. Emit one structured finding per missing-or-out-of-order sibling, naming the sibling and the diff.

## Implementation home

Lives under `livespec/.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/` per the existing doctor static-invariant pattern. Registered in the static-invariant registry per the v007 G8 static-enumeration discipline.

## Why both in-repo AND cross-repo enforcement

- In-repo `aggregate_completeness` (li-aggchk) catches normal drift: someone adds a check to dev-tooling, consumer's `just check` goes red until wired.
- Cross-repo invariant catches adversarial drift: someone edits a sibling's justfile to remove `check-aggregate-completeness` itself (disabling the in-repo gate). Without the cross-repo backstop, this would silently re-open the entire drift class.

## Acceptance

- Invariant fires fail when run against a sibling whose justfile lacks any canonical slug or has them out of order.
- Invariant fires fail when a sibling is registered in `cross_repo_targets` but has no resolvable justfile (treats as unknown → fail per the typed-union no-stalled-epic pattern).
- Integration test covering all three resolution paths: local_clone, GitHub-query, unresolvable.
title: livespec: add doctor wiring-completeness-cross-repo invariant

Phase 2.4 of epic li-univck. Target repo: livespec (this repo).

## Scope

Bump-pin livespec's `pyproject.toml` to `livespec-dev-tooling v0.3.0` and wire any missing canonical checks into `just check`. Low — livespec is the originator of the discipline; should be green or near-green. Any Red→Green cleanup is incidental.

- `just check` green on master.
- `check-no-stale-revise-branches` runs in CI.
- `check-aggregate-completeness` runs in CI and validates the aggregate against canonical.

description:
Surfaced by check-no-stale-revise-branches once wired into livespec's canonical aggregate (li-livwir, epic li-univck self-host).

## Background

The newly-wired canonical check check-no-stale-revise-branches refuses to pass while local branch refs prefixed `spec/*` are ahead of origin/master. Inspection found 4 stale local-ref branches whose tip commits have all been cherry-picked into origin/master under different SHAs (the canonical livespec revise flow lands a cherry-pick of the spec/* tip, not a merge):

- spec/recast-layer-3-livespec-only (1 commit ahead; subject matches master e04afb3)
- spec/recast-layer-3-revise (4 commits ahead; tip subject matches master cfef952)
- spec/rename-loop-to-orchestrate (3 commits ahead; tip subject matches master 603c3ff)
- spec/revise-wiring-completeness (1 commit ahead; tip subject matches master 413680e)

All 4 are orphan refs whose work has already landed; safe to delete.

## Scope

1. Verify each tip via `git log --grep` on subject against origin/master to confirm cherry-pick landing.
2. Delete each local branch ref via `git branch -D <name>`.
3. Re-run `just check-no-stale-revise-branches` to confirm green.

## Acceptance

- check-no-stale-revise-branches exits 0 from a fresh clone or from the primary checkout.
- No spec/* branches remain locally that aren't actively in use by an open worktree.

## Notes

li-livwir's PR merges with check-no-stale-revise-branches in the failed list (1 of 2 known failures, the other being a comment-no-historical-refs typo fixed in-line). The CI matrix entry for check-no-stale-revise-branches may need to gate appropriately, or the check may need to be made non-CI (it operates on local branch refs which are not meaningfully populated in CI clones).
title: Delete stale spec/* branches surfaced by check-no-stale-revise-branches

description:
Coordinating epic for revisiting each `just check` carve-out flag (env var gates, --allow-* flags, no-args short-circuits) that accumulated during the li-univck epic. Per user memory `feedback_carve_out_ratio_signals_mis_scope`: when carve-outs >= invariants, the invariants are mis-scoped. Each sub-task targets a specific carve-out and explores whether the invariant can be reshaped so the gate becomes unnecessary.

## Acceptance for every sub-task

Propose a concrete reshape that eliminates the carve-out, OR close as `resolution: "wontfix"` with explicit human approval (NOT autonomously by sub-agent). The "wontfix" path requires the human to be presented with the reshape options and explicitly reject all of them.

## Sub-tasks

- `li-cvtodo` -- Eliminate `LIVESPEC_RELEASE_GATE` env var (gates `check-no-todo-registry` and likely `check-no-lloc-soft-warnings`).
- `li-cvstale` -- Eliminate `--allow-stale-branches` flag (gates `check-no-stale-revise-branches` in non-revise contexts).
- `li-cvnoarg` -- Eliminate `*args` no-args short-circuit (gates `check-check-coverage-incremental` and `check-red-green-replay` when invoked from the aggregate with no args).
- `li-cvredmd` -- Eliminate `LIVESPEC_PRECOMMIT_RED_MODE` env var (gates `check-coverage` during Red commits in the Red-Green-Replay rhythm).

The patterns are wired into all 4 family repos' justfiles (livespec, livespec-dev-tooling, livespec-impl-plaintext, livespec-runtime). Eliminating a carve-out usually requires changes in `livespec-dev-tooling` (the canonical check) plus cleanup in each consumer's justfile.
title: Audit + eliminate justfile carve-out flags across the family

description:
Eliminate the `LIVESPEC_RELEASE_GATE` env var that conditionally runs `check-no-todo-registry` (and likely `check-no-lloc-soft-warnings` -- verify when picked up) only at release time.

## Current state

The check enforces "no TODO/FIXME entries in the codebase" as a release hygiene gate. But `tests/heading-coverage.json` uses literal `"TODO"` as placeholder test-IDs (the v064 governed-replacement pattern -- every new H2 in the spec adds a TODO entry that gets replaced when real test IDs are authored). Result: the check unconditionally fires on every per-commit run. Workaround: the justfile recipe checks `[[ -n "${LIVESPEC_RELEASE_GATE:-}" ]]` and short-circuits when the env var is unset. Wired in 4 family repos.

## Reshape directions to explore (not exhaustive)

1. **Sentinel-value reshape.** Change the heading-coverage placeholder from literal `"TODO"` to something the no-todo check doesn't pattern-match -- e.g., `"PENDING-TEST-ID"` or `"<unfilled>"`. Eliminates the conflict at the source.
2. **File-glob exclusion.** The no-todo check excludes `tests/heading-coverage.json` (and any similar known-placeholder files) by config. The check's contract becomes: "no TODO in source/spec/docs, EXCEPT in machine-managed registries with their own governed-replacement loop."
3. **Pattern strictness.** Tighten the TODO regex to require a specific format (e.g., `TODO(name):` with colon) that the heading-coverage placeholders don't match.
4. **Two-mode check.** Genuinely split into `check-no-todo-registry-strict` (release gate, fires on anything TODO-like) and `check-no-todo-registry` (per-commit, allows machine-managed placeholders). But this is the "Nth carve-out" anti-pattern -- would only land if 1-3 are rejected.

## Wontfix criteria

Closure as wontfix requires the human to be presented with reshape options 1-3 above, judge each, and explicitly reject all of them. The autonomous sub-agent picking this up MAY draft a reshape PR for review but MUST halt at PR opening for human decision; it MUST NOT merge the wontfix close autonomously.
title: Reshape no-todo-registry to eliminate LIVESPEC_RELEASE_GATE

description:
Eliminate the `--allow-stale-branches` flag that downgrades `check-no-stale-revise-branches` from `fail` to info-level when invoked from `just check` (vs. the load-bearing enforcement at `/livespec:revise` pre-step).

## Current state

The check enumerates local `refs/heads/spec/*` branches that are ahead of `origin/master`. Correct behavior at `/livespec:revise` pre-step (stale revise branches mean you're about to cut a new version against an outdated snapshot). Per-commit false-positive on every contributor machine that happens to have local spec branches checked out (typical when in-flight revise work is in a worktree). Workaround: the per-commit recipe passes `--allow-stale-branches` to downgrade the finding.

## Reshape directions to explore

1. **Context-scoped invariant.** Drop the check from `just check` entirely. The load-bearing enforcement is already at `/livespec:revise` pre-step (mandatory there). The per-commit run adds noise without enforcement value because of the `--allow-stale-branches` downgrade.
2. **PR-status-aware predicate.** Refine the heuristic: only fail on branches with NO open PR. Branches with open PRs are in-flight and expected to be ahead of master; branches without are likely abandoned. Eliminates false positives without losing detection.
3. **Primary-checkout-only predicate.** The check fires only when invoked against the bare-flag primary checkout (where no user-edited branches should exist). Secondary worktrees can freely carry spec/* branches. Aligns with the bare-flag invariant.
4. **`origin/spec/*` not `refs/heads/spec/*`.** Walk remote-tracked branches instead of local. Eliminates false-positive on local-only branches; catches genuinely-published-but-unmerged staleness.

## Wontfix criteria

Same as li-cvtodo -- human-approval required, sub-agent halts at PR open.
title: Reshape no-stale-revise-branches to eliminate --allow-stale-branches

description:
Eliminate the `*args` no-args short-circuit pattern in `check-check-coverage-incremental` and `check-red-green-replay`. Both checks are designed for hook-context invocation (changed-files list, commit-msg file path) and short-circuit to no-op when invoked from the canonical `just check` aggregate with no args.

## Current state

Both recipes have the shape: `if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then echo "::no-args short-circuit::"; exit 0; fi`. This makes them invocable in the canonical aggregate (where they no-op) AND in their proper context (lefthook hooks with the args they need). But the no-op invocation is dead work -- the canonical aggregate "wires" them but they accomplish nothing.

## Reshape directions to explore

1. **Recipes derive args from current state.** Instead of short-circuiting, the no-args mode infers args. `check-check-coverage-incremental` with no args could derive the changed-file list from `git diff origin/master...HEAD`. `check-red-green-replay` with no args could read the latest commit's message from `git log -1 --format=%B`. Eliminates the short-circuit; actually does useful work in the aggregate context.
2. **Two named recipes.** Split each check into `<slug>-hook` (takes args, invoked from lefthook) and `<slug>-aggregate` (no args, derives state). Only `<slug>-aggregate` lands in the canonical set; `<slug>-hook` stays as a livespec-private check.
3. **Remove from canonical set.** These checks are pre-commit-hook-shaped, not aggregate-shaped. Maybe they shouldn't be in the canonical-check-by-intent partition at all. Define a new "pre-commit-hook canonical" partition vs. the per-commit-aggregate canonical set. This requires a propose-change against `contracts.md` section "Shared code sync -- livespec-dev-tooling".
4. **New invariant.** Add a check that asserts every canonical-set member is invocable with no args AND does useful work (i.e., short-circuit-to-no-op is forbidden in the canonical set). This makes the carve-out impossible to add in the future; existing carve-out members must be either reshaped or moved out of canonical.

## Wontfix criteria

Same -- human approval required.
title: Reshape canonical aggregate to eliminate no-args short-circuits

description:
Eliminate the `LIVESPEC_PRECOMMIT_RED_MODE` env var that gates `check-coverage` during Red commits in the Red-Green-Replay rhythm.

## Current state

Red-Green-Replay's Red commit is test-only (intentionally failing, no impl). 100% coverage on a Red commit is incoherent (the impl that would be covered doesn't exist yet). Workaround: the red-green-replay commit hook sets `LIVESPEC_PRECOMMIT_RED_MODE=1` when committing a Red; `check-coverage` checks for this env var and short-circuits. Coverage is verified at the Green amend instead.

## Reshape directions to explore

1. **Internal plumbing, not a user-facing carve-out.** The env var is set BY the red-green-replay tool when invoking commit; users never set it manually. If it's purely an internal handshake between RGR and the coverage check, maybe it should ride on a more structured channel (e.g., a commit trailer that the check reads from `git log -1 --format=%B`). User-facing surface is then zero. (Note: this might still be a "carve-out" by some definitions, but moves the conditional logic into the check itself rather than the justfile recipe.)
2. **Coverage measured against Green snapshot.** Redefine the coverage invariant: coverage is verified against the working tree as-of-the-last-Green-state, not the current-commit state. Red commits land between Green states and don't add to the coverage measurement at all. Requires the check to know which commits are Green; could use the RGR trailers as the signal.
3. **Move coverage out of per-commit into post-Green-amend hook.** Don't run `check-coverage` on every commit. Run it on the Green amend specifically (lefthook post-amend). The Red commit phase is uncovered by design; that's not a gap, that's the RGR contract.
4. **Two-commit-atomically.** Red-Green-Replay treats the Red+Green pair as one logical commit. Maybe `check-coverage` should also treat them atomically -- defer the coverage measurement until the Green amend lands and then measure the cumulative state. This is option 2 framed differently.

## Wontfix criteria

Same -- human approval required.
title: Reshape coverage check to eliminate LIVESPEC_PRECOMMIT_RED_MODE

description:
Phase 1 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: livespec (SPECIFICATION/).

File a propose-change against `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag enforcement" (and any related sections) replacing the bare-flag invariant with the commit-refuse-hook invariant. Run revise to cut the next vNNN.

**Proposed new contract:**

1. Every livespec-governed primary checkout MUST have a populated working tree tracking origin/master via normal `git pull --ff-only`.
2. Every primary checkout MUST install a `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `.git/hooks/pre-push`) hook that refuses to run when `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` equals the configured primary path. The hook script body is a small portable shell snippet that exits 1 with a clear "use a worktree" message.
3. Worktrees share the `.git/` directory and inherit the hook script. Because the toplevel check fails inside a worktree, the hook is a no-op there — commits at worktrees proceed normally.
4. `just bootstrap` MUST install the commit-refuse hook idempotently. Re-running bootstrap MUST NOT duplicate the hook or overwrite an existing customized hook without warning.
5. The canonical check `primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` (replacing `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set`) MUST be wired in every consumer per the wiring-completeness invariant.

**Sections to update:**

- `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag enforcement" → renamed/rewritten as §"Primary-checkout commit-refuse hook"
- `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag bootstrap procedure" → renamed/rewritten to describe the hook-installation bootstrap step
- Any cross-references in `contracts.md`, `spec.md`, or other spec files

**Heading-coverage co-edit:** If H2 headings change, update `tests/heading-coverage.json` in lockstep per `.claude/CLAUDE.md` §"Revise co-edit discipline".

**Acceptance:** new vNNN cut with the rewritten contract; PC and paired revision file archived; doctor static green.
title: Propose+revise: replace bare-flag invariant with commit-refuse-hook invariant

description:
Phase 2 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

In `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/`:

1. Remove `primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py` (and corresponding tests).
2. Add `primary_checkout_commit_refuse_hook_installed.py`: walks the configured primary path (resolved per existing project-path discipline), verifies `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists, is executable, and matches the canonical hook body (or contains the expected toplevel-check + exit-1 pattern). Exit 0 when installed correctly; exit 4 with structured finding when absent or stale.
3. Add tests covering: hook installed correctly, hook missing, hook stale/different content, primary path resolution edge cases.
4. Update `canonical_checks.py` discovery — the new check appears automatically because the file lives under `checks/`.
5. Release v0.5.0 via release-please (MINOR bump: canonical set changed by removing one slug and adding another; technically removal is a breaking change for any consumer that had the old slug wired, but per the epic structure all consumers update in Phase 3, so the breakage is coordinated).

**Acceptance:** new dev-tooling release published with the flipped check; `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` lists `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` and no longer lists `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set`.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: remove bare-flag check, add commit-refuse-hook check, release v0.5.0

description:
Phase 3 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: `livespec`.

Mechanical conversion:

1. `git -C /data/projects/livespec config --unset core.bare` — drop the bare flag.
2. `git -C /data/projects/livespec checkout master -- .` — populate the working tree with current master content.
3. Verify the working tree matches origin/master: `git status` should show clean (or only show known-gitignored artifacts).
4. Install commit-refuse hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `pre-push`): a small shell script that checks `[[ "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" == "/data/projects/livespec" ]]` and exits 1 with a helpful message when true. The hook MUST be a no-op when run from a worktree (the toplevel check fails there).
5. Bump dev-tooling pin in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.uv.sources]` from v0.4.0 to v0.5.0; `uv lock`.
6. Update `justfile` `check:` recipe: replace `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` with `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` (alphabetical position changes accordingly).
7. Run `just check` end-to-end. Critically: `check-aggregate-completeness` MUST pass against the new aggregate. `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` MUST pass (the hook is installed and current).
8. Sanity test: from the primary path, attempt `git commit --allow-empty -m 'test'` — the hook MUST refuse. From a worktree, the same command MUST succeed.

**Halt conditions:** working tree population surfaces uncommitted edits the primary holds (shouldn't happen, but if so, halt and report); hook script doesn't behave correctly under one of the test cases; CI surfaces any unexpected drift from the bare-flag-to-normal conversion.

**Acceptance:** PR merged; `core.bare` unset; working tree populated and current; hook installed; pin bumped; aggregate green.
title: livespec: drop bare flag, install commit-refuse hook, bump dev-tooling v0.5.0

description:

description:
Phase 4 of epic li-unbare. Target: all 4 repos.

Update each repo's `just bootstrap` recipe to:

1. Replace the existing bare-flag-set step with: install the commit-refuse hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `.git/hooks/pre-push`. Idempotent: re-running bootstrap doesn't duplicate or overwrite a user-customized hook without warning.
2. Add a sanity check at the end of bootstrap that verifies `git commit --allow-empty -m 'bootstrap-sanity'` from the primary path refuses (immediately reverts the commit if it somehow lands, to keep history clean).
3. Remove any other lingering bare-flag bootstrap logic.

May be filed as 4 small PRs (one per repo) or one coordinated PR per repo. Mirror the established bump-pin propagation pattern.

**Acceptance:** fresh-clone-and-bootstrap on each repo produces the steady-state primary configuration (commit-refuse hook installed, working tree populated, core.bare unset). No leftover bare-flag logic in any bootstrap recipe.
title: Update just bootstrap recipe across 4 repos to install commit-refuse hook

description:
Phase 5 of epic li-unbare. Final exhaustive verification. Walk all 4 family repos plus the auto-memory directory plus any other artifact and verify NO leftover bare-flag state or references remain.

**Verification matrix:**

| Aspect | Check | Expected state |
|---|---|---|
| core.bare state | `git -C <repo> config --get core.bare` on all 4 primaries | unset (or `false`) on all |
| Working tree population | `git -C <repo> status --porcelain` on all 4 primaries | clean except gitignored cache (no missing tracked files) |
| Working tree content | content matches origin/master for tracked files | byte-identical for spot-checked files (CLAUDE.md, justfile, work-items.jsonl) |
| Commit-refuse hook | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `pre-push` exist, executable, match canonical body | all 4 primaries |
| Worktree no-op | A test commit from a worktree succeeds; same from primary refuses | all 4 |
| dev-tooling canonical check | `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` | lists `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed`, does NOT list `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` |
| Consumer justfiles | grep each repo's justfile for old slug `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` | zero matches |
| Consumer justfiles (new) | grep each repo's justfile for new slug | exactly one match in each |
| dev-tooling pin | grep each consumer's pyproject.toml for `livespec-dev-tooling` pin | v0.5.0+ in all |
| Spec files | grep `SPECIFICATION/` and `templates/*/SPECIFICATION/` in all 4 repos for `bare-flag\|bare flag\|core.bare` | zero matches (or only matches inside `history/vNNN/` archive directories, which are immutable) |
| non-functional-requirements + contracts | grep `non-functional-requirements.md`, `contracts.md` for bare-flag | only inside `history/` archive |
| Bootstrap recipes | grep each `justfile` and any `just bootstrap` body for bare-flag logic | zero matches |
| CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md | grep prose for `bare-flag\|bare flag\|core.bare` | zero matches |
| README files | grep all README.md files in 4 repos | zero matches |
| Auto-memory | `/home/ubuntu/.claude/projects/-data-projects-livespec/memory/feedback_bare_flag_use_git_show_not_filesystem.md` | removed (the rule no longer applies; primary reads are fresh) |
| Auto-memory | `feedback_worktree_discipline_mechanical_enforcement.md` | rewritten to point to the commit-refuse hook as the mechanism (NOT bare flag) |
| Auto-memory | MEMORY.md index | updated to reflect the above |
| Doctor static checks | grep `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/` for bare-flag references | zero matches outside archive |
| Wiring-completeness check Path A | verify `git -C ... show HEAD:justfile` is still correct (now also works on populated working trees because they're current; but the git-db path is still the contract) | no regression |
| tmp/ files | review `tmp/loop-status.md` and `tmp/prompt.md` for bare-flag references | optionally update for clarity (tmp/ is user-owned scratch per memory) |

**Methodology:**

1. Generate the verification report: walk every cell of the matrix, capture pass/fail with evidence (command output snippet).
2. Any failing cell: fix it inline (small) or file as follow-up (large), with explicit justification for the deferral.
3. Produce a final summary PR that lands any inline fixes + the audit report (as a comment or a tmp/ file).
4. Close `li-unbarsweep` only when EVERY cell passes or has an explicit, justified deferral.

**Acceptance:** the verification matrix is 100% green (or every yellow has explicit user-approved deferral); the auto-memory index reflects the new reality; the bare-flag concept exists only inside `history/vNNN/` archives across all 4 repos.
title: Exhaustive sweep: no leftover bare-flag state or references in any repo, spec, or memory

description:
Coordinating epic to replace the family-wide bare-flag invariant with a commit-refuse hook. Today every primary checkout sets `core.bare = true`, which forbids ALL working-tree operations and causes silent stale-read bugs (the on-disk content at the primary is frozen at pre-bare-flag and never refreshes). The actual intent is "no commits at primary" — which is a smaller hammer.

**New invariant.** Each primary clone is a normal working-tree clone tracking origin/master. A `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `pre-push`) hook checks whether `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` equals the configured primary path and exits 1 with a helpful message when so. Worktrees share `.git/` so they inherit the hook script, but their toplevel differs from the primary's, so the hook is a no-op for them. Result: reads, fetches, and pulls at the primary just work; commits refuse with a clear redirect to use a worktree.

**Phases:**

- **Phase 1 — Spec.** `li-unbarspec`: propose+revise the new contract in `non-functional-requirements.md` (or wherever the bare-flag invariant lives). Replaces the bare-flag section with the commit-refuse-hook section.
- **Phase 2 — Canonical check flip.** `li-unbardct`: in livespec-dev-tooling, remove `primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py` and add `primary_checkout_commit_refuse_hook_installed.py`. Bump dev-tooling release (v0.5.0).
- **Phase 3 — Per-repo conversion (parallel).** `li-unbarliv` (livespec), `li-unbarldt` (livespec-dev-tooling), `li-unbaripx` (livespec-impl-plaintext), `li-unbarrun` (livespec-runtime). Each: unset `core.bare`, repopulate working tree from origin/master, install commit-refuse hook, bump dev-tooling pin, update justfile aggregate (drop old slug, add new slug — still alphabetical canonical position).
- **Phase 4 — Bootstrap recipe.** `li-unbarboot`: update `just bootstrap` recipe across all 4 repos to install the commit-refuse hook on fresh clones (replacing the bare-flag bootstrap step). Fresh-clone path matches steady-state.
- **Phase 5 — Exhaustive sweep.** `li-unbarsweep`: walk all 4 repos exhaustively. Verify (a) `core.bare` is unset on each primary, (b) working trees are populated and match master, (c) commit-refuse hook is installed and fires correctly at primary but is a no-op at worktrees, (d) justfiles invoke the new canonical slug, (e) no SPECIFICATION/ files or `non-functional-requirements.md` text still references the old bare-flag invariant, (f) no `.claude/CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` prose references bare-flag-bootstrap, (g) the obsolete memories (`feedback_bare_flag_use_git_show_not_filesystem`, related) are removed or rewritten, (h) the `feedback_worktree_discipline_mechanical_enforcement` memory is rewritten to point to the commit-refuse hook as the mechanism. No leftover bare-flag state or references anywhere.
title: Replace bare-flag invariant with commit-refuse hook (un-bare the family)

description:
## Scope

Propose-change against `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` introducing a new section (working title: `## CLI end-to-end harness contract`) that mandates a top-of-pyramid user-surface end-to-end test for every plugin in the livespec family.

## Why this contract exists

Today's `## E2E harness contract` (lines ~674-754) drives wrapper scripts as Python subprocesses anchored at `_REPO_ROOT/.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/`. That's a *wrapper-surface* test, not a *user-surface* test. It does not exercise the `claude` CLI binary, plugin marketplace install, plugin loading, or slash-command dispatch as a real end user does. Install-shape bugs (missing vendored runtime, broken SKILL.md invocation paths, etc.) are invisible to it.

## Normative requirements the new contract MUST encode

1. **Sole entry point is the `claude` CLI binary.** Setup pre-populates a tmp `HOME` with `~/.claude/settings.json` declaring the marketplace + enabled plugin (or runs `claude -p "/plugin install ..."` as the first step). Workflow steps are `claude -p` subprocess invocations issuing slash commands (`--continue`/`--resume <id>` for multi-turn). No reach-around to wrapper Python files. No knowledge of cache layout. No claude-agent-sdk programmatic surface; the SDK is the wrapper-chain tier, not this tier.
2. **Pluggable implementation.** The harness drives an upstream `livespec` plugin in lockstep with one *impl* plugin (today `livespec-impl-plaintext`; tomorrow alternate implementations). The impl-plugin id is a parameter to the harness. The spec-side skill set is fixed; the impl-side skill set is whatever the installed impl plugin exposes.
3. **Structural skill discovery.** Skill enumeration walks `<installed-plugin>/skills/*/SKILL.md` in each plugin's installed location. Plugin slash-command prefix is read from `plugin.json`'s `name` field. No parallel manifest file; the Claude Code plugin directory structure is the canonical source of truth.
4. **Per-skill fixtures as a parallel filesystem convention.** A test fixtures directory (suggested layout: `<consumer-repo>/tests/e2e-cli/fixtures/<skill>/`) holds a `prompt.md` (text piped to `claude -p`) and an `expected_files.txt` (paths that must exist after) per skill. Discovery walks the same way: directory == fixture exists.
5. **Time-bomb coverage gate (fail-closed).** The harness MUST assert `discovered_skills - fixtured_skills == empty_set` and fail the test run otherwise. New skill added to either plugin trips the gate until either (a) a fixture directory is added or (b) the skill is explicitly listed in an `EXEMPT_SKILLS` table in the consumer repo with a written justification.
6. **Single canonical implementation in `livespec-dev-tooling`.** The harness itself (driver, fixtures loader, discovery, coverage gate) ships from `livespec-dev-tooling` and is consumed by every plugin repo via the existing pin-bump dependency flow. Each consumer repo wires the imported test function into its own pytest collection.
7. **Consumer obligations.** Each plugin claiming livespec-family membership MUST: (a) be installable solely via the `claude` CLI plugin-install surface; (b) ship a `tests/e2e-cli/` directory with per-skill fixtures; (c) pass the imported harness against itself (and, where applicable, against the upstream `livespec` plugin paired in lockstep).

## Relationship to the existing `## E2E harness contract`

The existing wrapper-chain harness STAYS. It is the second-tier integration test (faster, deterministic, mock-LLM). The new contract adds a higher tier whose only entry point is the user-visible CLI. Both tiers coexist in CI; the new tier replaces neither.

## Acceptance

- New `## CLI end-to-end harness contract` section accepted into `contracts.md` via the normal propose-change → revise flow.
- Section is registered in `tests/heading-coverage.json`.
- Spec-side `## E2E harness contract` is amended to clarify it is the wrapper-chain tier (sibling to, not superset of, the new CLI tier).
title: Spec contract: top-of-pyramid CLI-driven e2e harness contract (epic li-e2ecli Phase 1)

description:
## Scope

Implement the harness defined by the Phase 1 contract amendment in the shared `livespec-dev-tooling` package, cut a release, and prepare consumers to bump-pin.

## What ships

1. **Driver:** a pytest test function (or pytest plugin entry point) consumers import via `from livespec_dev_tooling.testing.cli_e2e import test_workflow_full_round_trip`. Takes a config object naming the impl-plugin id, the marketplace coordinates, and the fixtures directory. Sets `HOME=$tmp`, writes `~/.claude/settings.json` declaring the marketplace + enabled plugin, optionally runs the install slash command, then walks discovered skills through `claude -p` invocations (with `--continue`/`--resume` for multi-turn).
2. **Discovery helper:** walks `<plugin_install_dir>/skills/*/SKILL.md`, reads `plugin.json` for the slash-command prefix, returns `{plugin_id: [skill_names]}`. No manifest file.
3. **Coverage gate:** asserts `discovered_skills - fixtured_skills - exempt_skills == set()`; fails with the missing-skill list when not.
4. **Step orchestrator:** for each discovered skill, looks up `<fixtures>/<skill>/{prompt.md,expected_files.txt}`, runs the prompt via `claude -p`, asserts files exist + exit code 0. Handles multi-turn via session resume.
5. **Wireable to existing `LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS` selector** so the new tier slots into the family-wide convention without inventing a new env-var dialect.

## Out of scope

- Consumer-side integration (Phase 3 + 4).
- Real-tier LLM-driven workflows. The new harness is structural — `claude -p` invocations with canned prompts; the LLM-driven equivalent can be a future extension.

## Acceptance

- New release of `livespec-dev-tooling` (v0.5.0 or whatever the next major slot is) publishing the harness surface.
- Self-test inside `livespec-dev-tooling` exercises the harness against a tiny single-skill fixture-plugin to prove the discovery + coverage-gate + driver loop work in isolation.
- API documented in `livespec-dev-tooling`'s README + the consumer-facing import path is stable.
title: Implement CLI e2e harness in livespec-dev-tooling + release (epic li-e2ecli Phase 2)

description:
## Scope

Bump-pin `livespec` to the new `livespec-dev-tooling` release (Phase 2), add a CI job that runs the harness parametrized across every known impl plugin.

## What changes in this repo

1. Bump-pin `livespec-dev-tooling` in `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`.
2. Add `tests/e2e-cli/` with per-skill fixtures for every `/livespec:*` skill (seed, propose-change, critique, revise, doctor, prune-history, help, next, ...). Fixtures cover the spec-side happy path.
3. Add a tiny registry of known impl plugins (today: just impl-plaintext) — a list of `(plugin_id, marketplace_url)` tuples consumed by a parametrized fixture.
4. Add a CI workflow (or extend an existing one) invoking the harness for each (spec, impl) pair. Headless `claude -p` only; no tmux. Job installs `claude` CLI via `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` (same step `e2e-real.yml` already uses).
5. Heading-coverage entry for the new contract section if not already added in Phase 1.
6. Confirm the time-bomb gate fires correctly when a deliberate fixture is removed (red baseline test).

## Acceptance

- New CI job (e.g. `e2e-cli.yml`) green on master.
- Adding a new skill to `livespec` without a fixture fails the new job loudly.
- `just check` (or whatever local equivalent) runs the new tier in mock-cheap form for local development.
title: Wire CLI e2e harness into livespec repo CI (epic li-e2ecli Phase 3)

description:
## Scope

Bump-pin `livespec-impl-plaintext` to the new `livespec-dev-tooling` release, wire the harness into its CI, surface and resolve the install-shape bugs the harness will immediately discover.

## What changes in this repo

1. Bump-pin `livespec-dev-tooling` in `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`.
2. Add `tests/e2e-cli/` with per-skill fixtures for every `/livespec-impl-plaintext:*` skill — every authored heavyweight skill and every thin-transport surface listed in `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md`.
3. Add a CI workflow invoking the imported harness against the impl-plaintext plugin (paired with upstream `livespec`).
4. Expect the first harness run to fail. The expected failure mode is the union of the two impl-plaintext bugs already filed (see §Side-effect closures below). Each underlying defect MUST be fixed before this sub-task can close.
5. Heading-coverage entry mirroring upstream.

## Side-effect closures (impl-plaintext work-items.jsonl)

The following two P1 bugs already filed in `livespec-impl-plaintext/work-items.jsonl` are *expected* to be discovered by the new harness on first run and MUST be closed as part of completing this sub-task:

- **`li-k5dadf`** — Plugin bundle ships without `_vendor/livespec_runtime/`. Every cross-repo skill (next, list-work-items, capture-spec-drift, ...) fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'livespec_runtime'` when invoked from a fresh cache install. The spec mandates the vendor layout at `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Vendored dependencies in `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/`"; `_vendor/` has never been committed at any SHA. Fixing this is the precondition for any `claude -p` skill invocation to succeed against the cache install path.
- **`li-m4q4h5`** — Every SKILL.md quotes wrapper paths in the `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/<name>.py` form. Claude Code's installer flattens `.claude-plugin/scripts/` → `scripts/` in the cache. Cache also omits `pyproject.toml`/`uv.lock`, so `uv run` has no fallback. Fixing this is the precondition for any SKILL.md invocation block to be executable from the installed location.

Once both bugs are fixed and verified, the harness should go green end-to-end against the impl-plaintext plugin. The two bug records are then closed with audit pointers to the PR that lands this sub-task.

## Acceptance

- New CI job green on impl-plaintext master.
- `li-k5dadf` and `li-m4q4h5` records closed with resolution audit pointing here.
- A real `claude /plugin install ...` from a fresh tmp `HOME` followed by `claude -p "/livespec-impl-plaintext:next"` succeeds end-to-end.
title: Wire CLI e2e harness into livespec-impl-plaintext repo (epic li-e2ecli Phase 4)

description:
Coordinating epic to add a *user-surface* end-to-end integration test tier that drives the `claude` CLI binary itself — installing plugins via the CLI's plugin surface and exercising skills via slash commands from a foreign tmp project root.

## Motivation

The existing `## E2E harness contract` (contracts.md ~L674-754) drives wrapper scripts as Python subprocesses anchored at `_REPO_ROOT/.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/`. That is a wrapper-surface test, not a user-surface test. It does not validate that an end user — whose only interaction surface is `claude /plugin install ...` followed by typing slash commands — can install a livespec-family plugin and run its skills successfully.

Two P1 bugs filed against `livespec-impl-plaintext` on 2026-05-28 surface the gap concretely: the published plugin bundle is missing `_vendor/livespec_runtime/` (`li-k5dadf`), and every SKILL.md quotes a wrapper path that doesn't survive the installer's directory flattening (`li-m4q4h5`). Both bugs are invisible to every test in every livespec-family repo today, because every test runs from the dev tree where the bugs don't manifest. A single CLI-driven end-to-end test would have caught both immediately.

## Architecture (mechanics, not prose)

- **Driver:** headless `claude -p` invocations from pytest, multi-turn via `--continue`/`--resume <id>`. No tmux (terminal scraping is brittle and adds no signal). No claude-agent-sdk programmatic surface (that's a parallel surface; we want the CLI binary itself).
- **Pluggable impl:** spec-side `/livespec:*` skills are fixed; impl-side `/<impl-plugin>:*` skills are parametrized. The same imported test runs against any (livespec, impl-plugin) pair.
- **Structural discovery:** walk `<installed-plugin>/skills/*/SKILL.md`; read `plugin.json` for the slash-command prefix. No manifest file — the Claude Code plugin directory layout IS the contract.
- **Time-bomb coverage gate:** harness fails closed if any discovered skill lacks a fixture directory. New skill → next CI run blows up until either a fixture lands or the skill is explicitly exempted with written justification.
- **Single canonical implementation:** ships from `livespec-dev-tooling`; every consumer repo imports it via the existing pin-bump dependency flow.

## Phases

- **Phase 1 — Spec contract.** `li-e2ecspec`: propose-change against `livespec` `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` adding the new `## CLI end-to-end harness contract` section. Mandates the entry-point discipline, pluggable-impl shape, structural discovery, fixture convention, and time-bomb coverage gate. Distinct from (and coexists with) the existing `## E2E harness contract`.
- **Phase 2 — Dev-tooling implementation.** `li-e2ecdt`: implement the harness (driver, discovery, coverage gate, step orchestrator) in `livespec-dev-tooling`. Cut a release. Self-tested in isolation against a tiny single-skill fixture-plugin.
- **Phase 3 — `livespec` consumer.** `li-e2eclv`: bump-pin dev-tooling; add `tests/e2e-cli/` fixtures for every `/livespec:*` skill; add CI job parametrized over known impl plugins.
- **Phase 4 — `livespec-impl-plaintext` consumer.** `li-e2ecpx`: bump-pin dev-tooling; add `tests/e2e-cli/` fixtures for every `/livespec-impl-plaintext:*` skill; wire CI; **first run will fail** by surfacing the two P1 bugs already filed, which then MUST be fixed before this sub-task closes.

## Side-effect closures (cross-repo)

Two existing impl-plaintext work-items are expected to be closed as a direct side effect of making the new harness pass in `livespec-impl-plaintext`:

- `li-k5dadf` (impl-plaintext) — Plugin bundle ships without `_vendor/livespec_runtime/`. Every cross-repo skill currently fails with `ModuleNotFoundError` from a fresh cache install. Phase 4 fixture invocations cannot succeed until this is fixed.
- `li-m4q4h5` (impl-plaintext) — Every SKILL.md quotes wrapper paths in the `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/...` source-repo form that does not survive Claude Code's installer flattening. Phase 4 fixture invocations cannot succeed until the documented invocation paths match the post-install layout.

These two records are not local dependencies of this epic (they live in a different repo's work-items store) but are explicitly tracked here so that closure of `li-e2ecpx` requires verifying both bugs are independently closed in impl-plaintext, with audit pointers cross-linking the resolution back to this epic.

## Ordering nuance

Phase 1 (spec contract) and Phase 2 (dev-tooling implementation) want to land roughly together because the contract describes the harness shape and the harness implementation references the contract. Suggested sequence: file the Phase 1 propose-change; in parallel begin Phase 2 implementation. Land Phase 1 via revise first (so the contract is committed); then release Phase 2; then Phase 3 and Phase 4 can proceed in parallel.

## Wontfix criteria

Human approval required before any sub-task may be closed; sub-agent work halts at PR open per `feedback-cross-repo-propose-change-must-merge` discipline.
title: Top-of-pyramid CLI-driven e2e harness for the livespec plugin family

description:
The `/livespec-orchestrate` Layer 3 driver's session-state surfacing step (per `.claude/skills/livespec-orchestrate/SKILL.md`) uses a bare `gh run list --branch master --limit 1` query to check master CI state per family repo. This misses red CI when a non-CI workflow has run more recently than the last CI failure. The livespec family runs several frequent non-CI workflows ("Auto-update behind PRs" on cron, "Pin freshness sweep", "Bump pin from sibling dispatch" on release events, "Release Please"); any of those can mask a red CI workflow run.

Observed real consequence: the 2026-05-28 li-unbare orchestration session missed a 13-hour-old livespec master CI red (the `doctor-wiring-completeness-cross-repo` check failing in CI from a pre-existing infrastructure gap). The missed signal triggered an unnecessary Phase 3a sub-agent halt mid-iteration and an architectural detour. Per [[master-red-is-not-deferral]] this was supposed to be the first halt.

Fix options:
- Option A (minimal): Update orchestrator SKILL.md prose to mandate `--workflow CI` when querying CI state. Doc-only; relies on agent compliance.
- Option B (structural): Add a thin-transport helper script under `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/` or `.claude/skills/livespec-orchestrate/scripts/` that the orchestrator calls to do the session-state survey correctly. Encodes the right query mechanically.
- Option C (best): Have the orchestrator call dev-tooling's existing `master_ci_green.py` check directly for each family repo (with the appropriate repo path arg) — it already encodes the right query semantics (`--workflow CI`). Reuse beats reimplementation.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Orchestrator's session-state survey CANNOT miss a red CI workflow run because a non-CI workflow ran more recently — verified by test or manual repro against current master.
2. Applied across all 4 family repos (or designed to scale to future siblings).
3. Mistake-pattern mechanically prevented, not just documented.

Related memory: [[master-ci-query-must-filter-workflow]]
title: orchestrator session-state survey misses red CI when non-CI workflow ran more recently

description:
Phase 1 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: livespec (SPECIFICATION/).

File a propose-change against `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag enforcement" (and any related sections) replacing the bare-flag invariant with the commit-refuse-hook invariant. Run revise to cut the next vNNN.

**Proposed new contract:**

1. Every livespec-governed primary checkout MUST have a populated working tree tracking origin/master via normal `git pull --ff-only`.
2. Every primary checkout MUST install a `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `.git/hooks/pre-push`) hook that refuses to run when `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` equals the configured primary path. The hook script body is a small portable shell snippet that exits 1 with a clear "use a worktree" message.
3. Worktrees share the `.git/` directory and inherit the hook script. Because the toplevel check fails inside a worktree, the hook is a no-op there — commits at worktrees proceed normally.
4. `just bootstrap` MUST install the commit-refuse hook idempotently. Re-running bootstrap MUST NOT duplicate the hook or overwrite an existing customized hook without warning.
5. The canonical check `primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` (replacing `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set`) MUST be wired in every consumer per the wiring-completeness invariant.

**Sections to update:**

- `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag enforcement" → renamed/rewritten as §"Primary-checkout commit-refuse hook"
- `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Bare-flag bootstrap procedure" → renamed/rewritten to describe the hook-installation bootstrap step
- Any cross-references in `contracts.md`, `spec.md`, or other spec files

**Heading-coverage co-edit:** If H2 headings change, update `tests/heading-coverage.json` in lockstep per `.claude/CLAUDE.md` §"Revise co-edit discipline".

**Acceptance:** new vNNN cut with the rewritten contract; PC and paired revision file archived; doctor static green.
reason: completed — Phase 1: spec v095 cut (replace-bare-flag-with-commit-refuse-hook PC + revision); contracts.md + non-functional-requirements.md transitioned to the commit-refuse-hook contract.
title: Propose+revise: replace bare-flag invariant with commit-refuse-hook invariant

description:
Phase 2 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: livespec-dev-tooling.

In `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/`:

1. Remove `primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py` (and corresponding tests).
2. Add `primary_checkout_commit_refuse_hook_installed.py`: walks the configured primary path (resolved per existing project-path discipline), verifies `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists, is executable, and matches the canonical hook body (or contains the expected toplevel-check + exit-1 pattern). Exit 0 when installed correctly; exit 4 with structured finding when absent or stale.
3. Add tests covering: hook installed correctly, hook missing, hook stale/different content, primary path resolution edge cases.
4. Update `canonical_checks.py` discovery — the new check appears automatically because the file lives under `checks/`.
5. Release v0.5.0 via release-please (MINOR bump: canonical set changed by removing one slug and adding another; technically removal is a breaking change for any consumer that had the old slug wired, but per the epic structure all consumers update in Phase 3, so the breakage is coordinated).

**Acceptance:** new dev-tooling release published with the flipped check; `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` lists `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` and no longer lists `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set`.
reason: completed — Phase 2: dev-tooling check flipped (primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py removed, primary_checkout_commit_refuse_hook_installed.py added); v0.5.0 released (PRs #42/#46/#43). dev-tooling's own SPECIFICATION/contracts.md ported to the new check in PR #48 (v006) during the Phase 5 sweep.
title: livespec-dev-tooling: remove bare-flag check, add commit-refuse-hook check, release v0.5.0

description:
Phase 3 of epic li-unbare. Target repo: `livespec`.

Mechanical conversion:

1. `git -C /data/projects/livespec config --unset core.bare` — drop the bare flag.
2. `git -C /data/projects/livespec checkout master -- .` — populate the working tree with current master content.
3. Verify the working tree matches origin/master: `git status` should show clean (or only show known-gitignored artifacts).
4. Install commit-refuse hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `pre-push`): a small shell script that checks `[[ "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" == "/data/projects/livespec" ]]` and exits 1 with a helpful message when true. The hook MUST be a no-op when run from a worktree (the toplevel check fails there).
5. Bump dev-tooling pin in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.uv.sources]` from v0.4.0 to v0.5.0; `uv lock`.
6. Update `justfile` `check:` recipe: replace `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` with `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` (alphabetical position changes accordingly).
7. Run `just check` end-to-end. Critically: `check-aggregate-completeness` MUST pass against the new aggregate. `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed` MUST pass (the hook is installed and current).
8. Sanity test: from the primary path, attempt `git commit --allow-empty -m 'test'` — the hook MUST refuse. From a worktree, the same command MUST succeed.

**Halt conditions:** working tree population surfaces uncommitted edits the primary holds (shouldn't happen, but if so, halt and report); hook script doesn't behave correctly under one of the test cases; CI surfaces any unexpected drift from the bare-flag-to-normal conversion.

**Acceptance:** PR merged; `core.bare` unset; working tree populated and current; hook installed; pin bumped; aggregate green.
reason: completed — Phase 3: livespec primary converted (PRs #320/#321); core.bare unset, commit-refuse hook installed, dev-tooling pin v0.5.0, justfile slug flipped, obsolete plugin-doctor bare-flag check removed, wiring-completeness-cross-repo CI fixed.
title: livespec: drop bare flag, install commit-refuse hook, bump dev-tooling v0.5.0
