description:
Phase 4 of epic li-unbare. Target: all 4 repos.

Update each repo's `just bootstrap` recipe to:

1. Replace the existing bare-flag-set step with: install the commit-refuse hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `.git/hooks/pre-push`. Idempotent: re-running bootstrap doesn't duplicate or overwrite a user-customized hook without warning.
2. Add a sanity check at the end of bootstrap that verifies `git commit --allow-empty -m 'bootstrap-sanity'` from the primary path refuses (immediately reverts the commit if it somehow lands, to keep history clean).
3. Remove any other lingering bare-flag bootstrap logic.

May be filed as 4 small PRs (one per repo) or one coordinated PR per repo. Mirror the established bump-pin propagation pattern.

**Acceptance:** fresh-clone-and-bootstrap on each repo produces the steady-state primary configuration (commit-refuse hook installed, working tree populated, core.bare unset). No leftover bare-flag logic in any bootstrap recipe.
reason: completed — Phase 4: bootstrap recipe across all 4 repos installs the commit-refuse hook + livespec.primaryPath (no longer sets core.bare); verified via fresh-clone bootstrap smoke test (hook at pre-commit/pre-push/commit-msg, primary commit refused, core.bare stays unset) + commit-refuse sanity at all 4 primaries.
title: Update just bootstrap recipe across 4 repos to install commit-refuse hook

description:
Phase 5 of epic li-unbare. Final exhaustive verification. Walk all 4 family repos plus the auto-memory directory plus any other artifact and verify NO leftover bare-flag state or references remain.

**Verification matrix:**

| Aspect | Check | Expected state |
|---|---|---|
| core.bare state | `git -C <repo> config --get core.bare` on all 4 primaries | unset (or `false`) on all |
| Working tree population | `git -C <repo> status --porcelain` on all 4 primaries | clean except gitignored cache (no missing tracked files) |
| Working tree content | content matches origin/master for tracked files | byte-identical for spot-checked files (CLAUDE.md, justfile, work-items.jsonl) |
| Commit-refuse hook | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `pre-push` exist, executable, match canonical body | all 4 primaries |
| Worktree no-op | A test commit from a worktree succeeds; same from primary refuses | all 4 |
| dev-tooling canonical check | `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks --json` | lists `check-primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed`, does NOT list `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` |
| Consumer justfiles | grep each repo's justfile for old slug `check-primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` | zero matches |
| Consumer justfiles (new) | grep each repo's justfile for new slug | exactly one match in each |
| dev-tooling pin | grep each consumer's pyproject.toml for `livespec-dev-tooling` pin | v0.5.0+ in all |
| Spec files | grep `SPECIFICATION/` and `templates/*/SPECIFICATION/` in all 4 repos for `bare-flag\|bare flag\|core.bare` | zero matches (or only matches inside `history/vNNN/` archive directories, which are immutable) |
| non-functional-requirements + contracts | grep `non-functional-requirements.md`, `contracts.md` for bare-flag | only inside `history/` archive |
| Bootstrap recipes | grep each `justfile` and any `just bootstrap` body for bare-flag logic | zero matches |
| CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md | grep prose for `bare-flag\|bare flag\|core.bare` | zero matches |
| README files | grep all README.md files in 4 repos | zero matches |
| Auto-memory | `/home/ubuntu/.claude/projects/-data-projects-livespec/memory/feedback_bare_flag_use_git_show_not_filesystem.md` | removed (the rule no longer applies; primary reads are fresh) |
| Auto-memory | `feedback_worktree_discipline_mechanical_enforcement.md` | rewritten to point to the commit-refuse hook as the mechanism (NOT bare flag) |
| Auto-memory | MEMORY.md index | updated to reflect the above |
| Doctor static checks | grep `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/` for bare-flag references | zero matches outside archive |
| Wiring-completeness check Path A | verify `git -C ... show HEAD:justfile` is still correct (now also works on populated working trees because they're current; but the git-db path is still the contract) | no regression |
| tmp/ files | review `tmp/loop-status.md` and `tmp/prompt.md` for bare-flag references | optionally update for clarity (tmp/ is user-owned scratch per memory) |

**Methodology:**

1. Generate the verification report: walk every cell of the matrix, capture pass/fail with evidence (command output snippet).
2. Any failing cell: fix it inline (small) or file as follow-up (large), with explicit justification for the deferral.
3. Produce a final summary PR that lands any inline fixes + the audit report (as a comment or a tmp/ file).
4. Close `li-unbarsweep` only when EVERY cell passes or has an explicit, justified deferral.

**Acceptance:** the verification matrix is 100% green (or every yellow has explicit user-approved deferral); the auto-memory index reflects the new reality; the bare-flag concept exists only inside `history/vNNN/` archives across all 4 repos.
reason: completed — Phase 5 exhaustive sweep: all 4 primaries core.bare unset + populated + hook fires; justfiles wire new slug; live spec clean (dev-tooling contracts.md ported in PR #48 v006); 6 stale untracked PCs + 6 stale merged branches cleaned; obsolete memories rewritten; fixed a live core.bare=true regression on the livespec primary. Loose ends filed as follow-ups.
title: Exhaustive sweep: no leftover bare-flag state or references in any repo, spec, or memory

description:
Coordinating epic to replace the family-wide bare-flag invariant with a commit-refuse hook. Today every primary checkout sets `core.bare = true`, which forbids ALL working-tree operations and causes silent stale-read bugs (the on-disk content at the primary is frozen at pre-bare-flag and never refreshes). The actual intent is "no commits at primary" — which is a smaller hammer.

**New invariant.** Each primary clone is a normal working-tree clone tracking origin/master. A `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and `pre-push`) hook checks whether `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` equals the configured primary path and exits 1 with a helpful message when so. Worktrees share `.git/` so they inherit the hook script, but their toplevel differs from the primary's, so the hook is a no-op for them. Result: reads, fetches, and pulls at the primary just work; commits refuse with a clear redirect to use a worktree.

**Phases:**

- **Phase 1 — Spec.** `li-unbarspec`: propose+revise the new contract in `non-functional-requirements.md` (or wherever the bare-flag invariant lives). Replaces the bare-flag section with the commit-refuse-hook section.
- **Phase 2 — Canonical check flip.** `li-unbardct`: in livespec-dev-tooling, remove `primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py` and add `primary_checkout_commit_refuse_hook_installed.py`. Bump dev-tooling release (v0.5.0).
- **Phase 3 — Per-repo conversion (parallel).** `li-unbarliv` (livespec), `li-unbarldt` (livespec-dev-tooling), `li-unbaripx` (livespec-impl-plaintext), `li-unbarrun` (livespec-runtime). Each: unset `core.bare`, repopulate working tree from origin/master, install commit-refuse hook, bump dev-tooling pin, update justfile aggregate (drop old slug, add new slug — still alphabetical canonical position).
- **Phase 4 — Bootstrap recipe.** `li-unbarboot`: update `just bootstrap` recipe across all 4 repos to install the commit-refuse hook on fresh clones (replacing the bare-flag bootstrap step). Fresh-clone path matches steady-state.
- **Phase 5 — Exhaustive sweep.** `li-unbarsweep`: walk all 4 repos exhaustively. Verify (a) `core.bare` is unset on each primary, (b) working trees are populated and match master, (c) commit-refuse hook is installed and fires correctly at primary but is a no-op at worktrees, (d) justfiles invoke the new canonical slug, (e) no SPECIFICATION/ files or `non-functional-requirements.md` text still references the old bare-flag invariant, (f) no `.claude/CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` prose references bare-flag-bootstrap, (g) the obsolete memories (`feedback_bare_flag_use_git_show_not_filesystem`, related) are removed or rewritten, (h) the `feedback_worktree_discipline_mechanical_enforcement` memory is rewritten to point to the commit-refuse hook as the mechanism. No leftover bare-flag state or references anywhere.
reason: completed — Epic done: family-wide bare-flag invariant replaced with the commit-refuse-hook invariant across all 4 repos (spec v095 + dev-tooling v006; dev-tooling check v0.5.0; 4 primary conversions; bootstrap recipe; exhaustive sweep). All 8 sub-tasks closed.
title: Replace bare-flag invariant with commit-refuse hook (un-bare the family)

description: During epic li-unbare the livespec primary was repeatedly observed regressing to core.bare=true (most recently 2026-05-28, .git/config mtime 22:25). git 2.51 worktree add/remove/prune does NOT flip core.bare (empirical probe on a throwaway repo, AND confirmed: a livespec worktree add+remove during the sweep left core.bare unset). No committed recipe (justfile/bootstrap/CI) sets it. Suspected trigger: a stale-branch 'just bootstrap' (pre-Phase-3a recipe that still ran 'git config core.bare true') invoked from one of the leftover agent worktrees, or a manual set. The primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed doctor invariant catches core.bare=true as a safety net (contracts.md:192), so it is self-healing on the next doctor run, but the trigger should be root-caused. Investigation: forensic on what wrote .git/config at the regression time; audit leftover worktrees on pre-Phase-3a branches for stale bootstrap recipes. Related: the stale-worktree-hygiene follow-up.
title: Root-cause the core.bare transient re-set on the livespec primary (post-li-unbare)

description: 11 leftover agent worktrees accumulated under /data/projects/livespec/.claude/worktrees/ across prior orchestrator sessions (several locked, on stale branches whose content largely landed via different commits). During li-unbare one squatting worktree (agent-a40ff0cde3066585f) on master with a polluted index re-adding the deleted bare-flag check was removed, plus 6 merged worktree-agent-* branches were deleted. The remaining 11 worktrees should be audited (which carry genuinely-unmerged work vs are stale) and cleaned via git worktree remove. A stale-world worktree running an old 'just bootstrap' is a suspected trigger for the core.bare regression (see the core.bare root-cause follow-up).
title: Audit + clean 11 leftover agent worktrees under livespec/.claude/worktrees/

description:
## Scope

Propose-change against `livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" adding a sub-rule that strengthens the existing heading-coverage invariant for `scenarios.md` entries specifically: each scenario's mapped test MUST live at the **middle (integration) or top (e2e / CLI) tier** of the test pyramid, not at the unit tier.

## Current state

`constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" (lines 219-235) mandates that every `##` heading in a template-declared NLSpec file have an entry in `tests/heading-coverage.json` mapping to a pytest node id. The rule is uniform across spec files — a unit test at `tests/livespec/commands/test_seed.py::...` satisfies the gate for a `## Scenario: ...` heading just as it does for a `## Specification model` heading.

But scenarios are categorically different: scenarios.md describes user-observable behavior. A scenario covered only by a unit test isn't really covered — the unit test verifies internal shape, not the user-observable round trip the scenario describes. Today's gate accepts that substitution silently.

## Proposed amendment

Add a sub-rule to §"Heading taxonomy" along these lines:

> For entries whose `spec_file` is `scenarios.md`, the mapped `test` node id MUST > resolve to a test in the integration tier or above. Operationally: the node-id > path component MUST begin with one of the integration-tier prefixes (e.g., > `tests.e2e.…`, `tests.integration.…`, `tests.prompts.…`, …) enumerated in the > per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist; OR the resolved > test function MUST carry an explicit `pytest.mark.integration` (or stronger) > marker. `TODO` entries are still allowed during transition but MUST carry a > reason explicitly acknowledging the tier requirement.

The check `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.heading_coverage` MUST be extended to enforce this sub-rule, with a new diagnostic class (e.g., `scenario heading mapped to unit-tier test`).

## Why a per-repo allowlist

Different repos place integration tests in different directories (`tests/e2e/`, `tests/integration/`, `tests/consumer/`, `tests/prompts/`). The rule should be structural (a prefix list) rather than hard-coded so each repo can declare its own convention without amending the shared check.

## Out of scope

- Re-tier-ing any existing `scenarios.md` entries that currently point at unit-tier tests. That's per-repo cleanup work (see Phases 2-3 of this epic for the library repos; livespec itself is already mostly-compliant per the audit in the epic body).
- Forcing the same rule on `contracts.md`, `constraints.md`, or `spec.md` headings. Those legitimately may be exercised by unit-tier tests; only scenarios are user-observable-by-definition.

## Acceptance

- New sub-rule accepted into `constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" via propose-change → revise.
- `heading_coverage` check extended to enforce the scenario-tier rule, with the per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist mechanism.
- Dev-tooling release cut at the matching version; downstream repos can bump-pin to pick up the strengthened check.
- livespec's own `scenarios.md` entries audited: any currently pointing at unit-tier tests are re-pointed at e2e equivalents or moved to TODO with a tier-rationale reason.
title: Strengthen livespec heading-coverage to require middle/top-of-pyramid tests for scenarios.md (epic li-scetier Phase 1)

description:
## Scope

1. Propose-change against `livespec-runtime/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` declaring the heading-coverage invariant **independently** in the runtime spec, including the strengthened scenario-tier sub-rule from Phase 1.
2. Implement the consumer-style top-of-pyramid test tier in the runtime repo: tests that import the public `livespec_runtime.*` modules and exercise them the way actual consumers (livespec, livespec-impl-plaintext) do, against `tmp_path`-scoped cross-repo checkouts.
3. Wire every `scenarios.md` heading in the runtime spec to the new tier; fail-closed via the strengthened `heading_coverage` check.

## Why this can't be done by reference to livespec

`livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Forbidden: references OUTSIDE the same SPECIFICATION/ tree" (L245+) forbids the runtime spec from citing the livespec spec to inherit the invariant. The text has to be **independently restated** in the runtime spec, scoped to the runtime's own spec files. (The allowlist mechanism exists but is for cross-repo coordination citations, not for inheriting normative rules.)

## Library-shape top-of-pyramid

Unlike the CLI plugin family (where top-of-pyramid means driving `claude -p` from a foreign project root — see epic li-e2ecli), a library's top-of-pyramid is **live exercise of its public API as consumers consume it**. For livespec-runtime this means tests that:

- `import livespec_runtime.cross_repo.{types, resolve, errors}` (or whatever the public surface is at the time of implementation).
- Set up `tmp_path`-scoped checkouts mimicking the cross-repo arrangement consumers operate against.
- Drive complete consumer-shaped workflows (`resolve_ref(...)`, manifest reads, error-path handling) end-to-end.
- Assert on consumer-visible return values + error types, not on internal representations.

Suggested layout: `tests/consumer/` (or `tests/e2e/` to align with the family-wide convention). The choice is per-repo discretion; whichever layout is chosen MUST be registered in the repo's `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist introduced in Phase 1.

## Audit + retarget existing scenarios.md entries

Today's `livespec-runtime/tests/heading-coverage.json` may include `scenarios.md` entries pointing at unit-tier tests; this sub-task surveys them and retargets each to a consumer-style test in the new top-of-pyramid tier (or files a TODO with a tier-rationale reason if implementation is deferred).

## Acceptance

- New heading-coverage invariant section (or amendment to an existing section) accepted into `livespec-runtime/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` (or `non-functional-requirements.md`, whichever fits the existing layout).
- `tests/consumer/` (or equivalent) tier exists, with one test per `scenarios.md` scenario the spec exposes.
- `tests/heading-coverage.json` for `scenarios.md` entries all resolve to the new tier (or to a TODO with the tier-rationale reason).
- Bump-pin to the dev-tooling release shipping the strengthened check; `just check-heading-coverage` exits 0.
title: Declare heading-coverage invariant + add consumer-style top-of-pyramid tests in livespec-runtime (epic li-scetier Phase 2)

description:
## Scope

1. Propose-change against `livespec-dev-tooling/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` declaring the heading-coverage invariant **independently** in the dev-tooling spec, including the strengthened scenario-tier sub-rule from Phase 1.
2. Implement the consumer-style top-of-pyramid test tier in the dev-tooling repo: tests that import each check from `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.*` and run it against a fixture mini-project repo with deliberately-injected violations, asserting the check fires the expected diagnostic (and stays green when the violation is removed).
3. Wire every `scenarios.md` heading in the dev-tooling spec to the new tier; fail-closed via the strengthened `heading_coverage` check.

## Why this can't be done by reference to livespec

Same rationale as Phase 2 (`li-scetrn`). `livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Forbidden: references OUTSIDE the same SPECIFICATION/ tree" forbids dev-tooling's spec from citing the livespec spec to inherit the invariant. The text has to be independently restated in dev-tooling's spec.

## Library-shape top-of-pyramid

For livespec-dev-tooling, the public surface is the check modules under `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.*`. Top-of-pyramid is a **consumer-perspective** test harness:

- Materialize a tiny fixture project under `tmp_path` (a mini Python project with the relevant files the check inspects — `pyproject.toml`, `tests/`, a `SPECIFICATION/` if the check needs one, etc.).
- Import the check the same way a consumer's `justfile` does: `from livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug> import main` (or invoke as a subprocess via `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`).
- Run the check against the fixture project; assert it fires (or doesn't) per the scenario's expected outcome.
- For checks that have multiple failure modes, one consumer test per documented failure mode in `scenarios.md`.

Distinguish this from the dev-tooling repo's existing unit-tier check tests, which live alongside each check module and verify its internal helpers. The new tier runs the check **as a consumer would invoke it**.

Note that dev-tooling's checks already self-host: most run against this repo's own files as part of `just check`. The new tier is distinct from self-hosting — it isolates each check against a controlled fixture, eliminating coupling to the live repo state.

## Audit + retarget existing scenarios.md entries

Today's `livespec-dev-tooling/tests/heading-coverage.json` may include `scenarios.md` entries pointing at unit-tier tests; this sub-task surveys them and retargets each to a consumer-style test in the new tier.

## Acceptance

- New heading-coverage invariant section accepted into `livespec-dev-tooling/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` (or `non-functional-requirements.md`).
- `tests/consumer/` (or equivalent) tier exists, with one test per `scenarios.md` scenario.
- `tests/heading-coverage.json` for `scenarios.md` entries all resolve to the new tier.
- `just check-heading-coverage` exits 0 against the strengthened check.
title: Declare heading-coverage invariant + add consumer-style top-of-pyramid tests in livespec-dev-tooling (epic li-scetier Phase 3)

description:
Coordinating epic to (a) strengthen the existing heading-coverage invariant so that `scenarios.md` entries are required to map to middle/top-of-pyramid tests, not unit-tier tests, and (b) propagate the invariant — and the corresponding consumer-style top-of-pyramid test tier — to the two library repos in the family (`livespec-runtime`, `livespec-dev-tooling`).

## Motivation

`livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" (L219-235) already mandates that every `##` heading in a template-declared NLSpec file (including `scenarios.md`) have a `tests/heading-coverage.json` entry mapping to a pytest node id, mechanically enforced by `dev-tooling/checks/heading_coverage.py`. Audit confirms: all 20 of livespec's `scenarios.md` `##` headings are wired. **That's the existing invariant the user remembered.**

Two structural gaps:

1. **The invariant doesn't pin a pyramid tier.** A unit-tier test satisfies the gate for a scenario heading just as it does for a `## Specification model` heading. But scenarios.md is by definition user-observable behavior; a unit test is not really coverage. The gate accepts that substitution silently today.
2. **The libraries inherit the mechanism but not the invariant text.** Both `livespec-runtime` and `livespec-dev-tooling` have populated `tests/heading-coverage.json` files and run the shared `heading_coverage` check via the tooling pin — but neither library's own `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` (or `non-functional-requirements.md`) actually declares the invariant. A standalone reader of either library's spec tree wouldn't know it's a normative rule; the registry file looks like a tooling artifact with no spec backing. The §"Forbidden: references OUTSIDE the same SPECIFICATION/ tree" rule (L245+) explicitly forbids inheriting the invariant by reference; it has to be independently restated.

## Library top-of-pyramid shape

Distinct from the CLI plugin family (epic `li-e2ecli`): a library's top-of-pyramid is **live exercise of its public API as consumers consume it**, not driving the `claude` CLI binary. Concretely:

- `livespec-runtime`: import `livespec_runtime.*` modules, drive consumer-shaped workflows (cross-repo resolution, manifest reads, error paths) against `tmp_path`-scoped checkouts. Assert on consumer-visible return values + error types.
- `livespec-dev-tooling`: import each check from `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.*`, run it against a fixture mini-project under `tmp_path` with deliberately-injected violations, assert it fires the expected diagnostic. Distinct from the repo's existing self-hosting (where checks run against this repo's own files), which couples test outcomes to live repo state.

Each library's `scenarios.md` should describe its public API in user-observable terms. The strengthened invariant then forces each scenario to be exercised at the consumer-surface tier.

## Phases

- **Phase 1 — Strengthen the rule (livespec spec + dev-tooling check).** `li-scetlv`: propose-change against `livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" adding a scenarios.md-specific sub-rule that the mapped test MUST live at integration tier or above. Operationally: a per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist of node-id path prefixes (e.g., `tests.e2e`, `tests.integration`, `tests.consumer`, `tests.prompts`). The shared `heading_coverage` check is extended to enforce. Dev-tooling release cut.
- **Phase 2 — Runtime adoption.** `li-scetrn`: declare the invariant independently in `livespec-runtime/SPECIFICATION/`; implement a consumer-style top-of-pyramid tier (`tests/consumer/` or `tests/e2e/`); retarget every `scenarios.md` entry to the new tier. Bump-pin to the Phase 1 dev-tooling release.
- **Phase 3 — Dev-tooling adoption.** `li-scetdt`: declare the invariant independently in `livespec-dev-tooling/SPECIFICATION/`; implement consumer-style check-runner tier (each check invoked against a fixture mini-project); retarget every `scenarios.md` entry to the new tier. Bump-pin.

## Ordering nuance

Phase 1 lands first and the dev-tooling release ships before Phases 2-3 begin. Phases 2 and 3 can proceed in parallel after that. Each per-library phase couples the spec amendment with the impl retargeting work, because amending the spec without retargeting existing scenarios.md entries would put the repo into immediate violation of its own strengthened invariant.

## Relationship to li-e2ecli

Sibling, not parent-child. `li-e2ecli` adds a CLI-driven user-surface test tier for the plugin family (livespec, impl-plaintext). `li-scetier` adds an API-consumer-driven test tier for the library family (runtime, dev-tooling) plus tightens the existing scenarios coverage invariant to demand it. The two cover different artifact shapes (CLI binaries vs. Python imports) and apply to different repos.

## Wontfix criteria

Human approval required before any sub-task may close; sub-agent work halts at PR open per the cross-repo propose-change-must-merge discipline.
title: Scenario-tier coverage invariant + library top-of-pyramid testing

description:
The Layer 3 orchestrator orphaned 18 worktrees because: (1) it dispatched via the harness `isolation: "worktree"` mechanism, which only auto-removes UNCHANGED worktrees (any worktree carrying committed work becomes a permanent orphan); (2) `gh pr merge --auto` merges server-side AFTER the sub-agent exits, so the agent cannot self-clean; (3) nothing reconciled the leftovers afterward. This epic adds mechanical worktree-orphan cleanup: DETECTION (doctor `no-stale-worktree` case-b remote-gone) + a deterministic idempotent reaper ACTION + orchestrator wiring that USES the reaper.

**Sub-tasks:**

- **li-wtreap-wi1** — complete the doctor `no_stale_worktree` check so it covers case (b): a worktree whose branch is remote-gone (the rebase-merge signal), in addition to case (a) merged-into-default. DETECTION counterpart.
- **li-wtreap-wi2** — add the deterministic `reap-stale-worktrees` ACTION (`dev-tooling/reap_stale_worktrees.py` + `just reap-stale-worktrees [repo] [--dry-run]` recipe): removes non-primary worktrees whose branch is remote-gone/merged AND clean AND not live-locked; primary is never touched; idempotent.
- **li-wtreap-wi3** — wire the reaper into the orchestrator skill at three points (session start, post-merge, post-loop), drop the harness `isolation: "worktree"` dispatch in favor of self-managed sub-agent worktrees, and correct the orphan-causing post-merge self-clean fiction. Retain the core.bare re-verify+repair discipline.

Recurrence prevented end-to-end: the check surfaces orphans, the reaper removes them, the orchestrator runs the reaper unconditionally.
reason: completed — Epic done: worktree-orphan recurrence prevented end-to-end. DETECTION (doctor no_stale_worktree case-b remote-gone, PR #327) + ACTION (deterministic idempotent reap-stale-worktrees, PR #328) + orchestrator WIRING (self-managed dispatch, post-merge-self-clean fiction removed, reaper wired at session-start/post-merge/post-loop, core.bare guard retained — THIS PR). All 3 sub-tasks closed.
title: Mechanical worktree-orphan cleanup enforcement

description: Phase 1 (detection) of epic li-wtreap. The doctor `no_stale_worktree` check only covered case (a) — a worktree whose branch is merged into the default branch. The rebase-merge that `gh pr merge --auto --rebase` performs deletes the remote branch and leaves NO merged-into-default ancestry signal, so the orphaned worktrees from rebase-merges went undetected. This sub-task completes case (b): a worktree whose local branch is remote-gone (absent from `git ls-remote --heads origin <branch>`) is also stale. The check now fires on case (a) OR case (b). DETECTION layer only — it WARNS; the ACTION is the reaper (li-wtreap-wi2).
reason: completed — no_stale_worktree case-b remote-gone detection landed via PR #327 (merge commit 6c6cf12). The check now warns on case (a) merged-into-default OR case (b) remote-gone.
title: Complete no_stale_worktree case-b (remote-gone) detection in doctor

description: Phase 2 (action) of epic li-wtreap. Add the deterministic, idempotent reaper `dev-tooling/reap_stale_worktrees.py` plus the `just reap-stale-worktrees [repo] [--dry-run]` recipe. For every NON-primary worktree it reaps (`git worktree remove` + `git branch -D` + `git worktree prune`) IF AND ONLY IF the branch is remote-gone (the reliable rebase-merge signal) AND the working tree is clean AND it is not held by a live process lock. The primary is never touched; dirty/remote-present/live-locked/detached-HEAD worktrees are skipped; `--dry-run` reports the would-reap set without mutating. Operates on any family repo by path (`--repo <path>`), since the orchestrator is livespec-resident and reaps each repo. It is an ACTION tool, not part of `just check`.
reason: completed — reap-stale-worktrees action (dev-tooling/reap_stale_worktrees.py) + just reap-stale-worktrees recipe landed via PR #328 (merge commit e65ba42). Deterministic + idempotent.
title: Deterministic reap-stale-worktrees action + recipe

description: Phase 3 (wiring) of epic li-wtreap. Rewrite `.claude/skills/livespec-orchestrate/SKILL.md` to USE the detection + action layers: (1) drop the harness `isolation: "worktree"` dispatch — sub-agents now create their OWN secondary worktree via `git worktree add`, because the harness mechanism only auto-removes UNCHANGED worktrees (committed work -> permanent orphan) and has flipped `core.bare`; (2) delete the orphan-causing fiction that a sub-agent self-cleans its worktree post-merge (the merge is async/server-side, so the agent usually exits first and CANNOT self-clean) — the sub-agent now just reports the PR number + that auto-merge is armed; (3) wire `just reap-stale-worktrees` into the loop at three points — session start (Step 1, reconciles prior/crashed-session orphans), post-merge (Step 3c, removes the just-merged worktree), and post-loop (Step 4, final sweep); add a §"Worktree hygiene (reaper)" subsection; (4) KEEP the core.bare re-verify+repair discipline (still required even with self-managed worktrees — core.bare has regressed without harness isolation; tracked as li-iroguc). Doc/config changeset (zero .py) -> doc-only commit route, chore(...) prefix.
reason: completed — orchestrator reaper-wiring + creation-side fix landed via THIS PR (chore/li-wtreap-orchestrator-wiring). SKILL.md now dispatches self-managed worktrees, drops harness isolation:worktree, deletes the post-merge self-clean fiction, wires the reaper at session-start/post-merge/post-loop, and retains the core.bare re-verify+repair guard.
title: Wire reaper into orchestrator + fix worktree-creation guidance

description: 11 leftover agent worktrees accumulated under /data/projects/livespec/.claude/worktrees/ across prior orchestrator sessions (several locked, on stale branches whose content largely landed via different commits). During li-unbare one squatting worktree (agent-a40ff0cde3066585f) on master with a polluted index re-adding the deleted bare-flag check was removed, plus 6 merged worktree-agent-* branches were deleted. The remaining 11 worktrees should be audited (which carry genuinely-unmerged work vs are stale) and cleaned via git worktree remove. A stale-world worktree running an old 'just bootstrap' is a suspected trigger for the core.bare regression (see the core.bare root-cause follow-up).
reason: completed — 18 orphaned worktrees + 1 husk hand-cleaned across the family. Recurrence now prevented by epic li-wtreap: doctor no_stale_worktree case-b remote-gone detection (PR #327) + the deterministic reap-stale-worktrees action (PR #328) + orchestrator wiring that runs the reaper at session-start/post-merge/post-loop and dispatches self-managed worktrees instead of harness isolation:worktree.
title: Audit + clean 11 leftover agent worktrees under livespec/.claude/worktrees/

description: Eliminate the long-standing core.bare=true regression on the livespec primary. Root cause (pinned via li-iroguc): when git fires a commit hook inside a worktree it injects GIT_DIR=.git/worktrees/<name> (plus GIT_INDEX_FILE/GIT_WORK_TREE/GIT_PREFIX) into the hook environment; the commit-refuse wrapper delegated to `mise exec lefthook -- lefthook run` WITHOUT clearing that env, so lefthook misread the repo as bare and wrote core.bare=true into the shared .git/config. Proven A/B: the same lefthook command WITH the worktree GIT_DIR flips core.bare; WITHOUT it the flag stays unset; a wrapper that unsets the env leaves core.bare unset on a real commit. (Plain git is immune; it is lefthook.) This epic covers the wrapper fix (wi1), tightening the commit-refuse fingerprint to require the unset + --no-auto-install (wi2), and re-bootstrapping all four family primaries with the corrected wrapper (wi3).
title: Eliminate core.bare flip + clobber + detection gap

description: The commit-refuse / lefthook-dispatch wrapper (dev-tooling/git-hook-wrapper.sh + templates/impl-plugin/dev-tooling/git-hook-wrapper.sh) now unsets GIT_DIR GIT_INDEX_FILE GIT_WORK_TREE GIT_PREFIX on its own line immediately before the `exec mise exec lefthook -- lefthook run --no-auto-install` line. git injects GIT_DIR=<worktree-gitdir> into the hook env when a hook fires inside a worktree; lefthook run with that env misreads the repo as bare and writes core.bare=true into the shared .git/config (root cause li-iroguc). Both wrapper files carry the unset (kept in sync). A parametrized test in tests/dev-tooling/test_git_hook_wrapper.py asserts each wrapper contains the unset line AND that it precedes the lefthook-run exec line. End-to-end verified: a .py commit whose imports ruff autofixes still got the autofix re-staged into the commit with GIT_INDEX_FILE unset, and core.bare stayed unset throughout; shipped the full 4-variable unset variant.
reason: completed — wrapper unsets git-injected GIT_DIR/GIT_INDEX_FILE/GIT_WORK_TREE/GIT_PREFIX before lefthook (both wrapper files in sync) + paired test landed via THIS PR (fix/li-corebare-fix-wrapper-unset). Fix proven by the RCA A/B (lefthook WITH worktree GIT_DIR flips core.bare; WITHOUT stays unset) and verified end-to-end (ruff autofix re-staged with GIT_INDEX_FILE unset; core.bare stayed unset).
title: wrapper unsets git-injected env before lefthook

description: Follow-up (dev-tooling). The primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed invariant currently recognizes the wrapper body via the fingerprint substrings `# livespec commit-refuse hook`, `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, and `exit 1`. Tighten the fingerprint to ALSO require the `--no-auto-install` flag and the `unset GIT_DIR ...` line, so a stale or partially-reverted wrapper that drops either guard is caught by the doctor static check rather than silently regressing the core.bare flip / auto-sync clobber. Note the SKIP gap: the commit-refuse doctor check skips on a bare repo, so it cannot self-heal the very state it would detect; consider closing that gap here too.
title: tighten commit-refuse fingerprint to require --no-auto-install + unset

description: Follow-up (ops). Re-run `just bootstrap` on all four family primaries (livespec, livespec-impl-plaintext, livespec-dev-tooling, livespec-runtime) once the corrected wrapper (li-corebare-fix-wi1) has landed on each repo's master, so the installed .git/hooks/{pre-commit,pre-push,commit-msg} carry the unset line. The livespec primary already runs a locally-patched wrapper equivalent to the fix; the other three primaries still need the corrected wrapper installed. Until then those primaries remain vulnerable to the lefthook core.bare flip from worktree hook invocations.
title: re-bootstrap all four primaries with corrected wrapper

description: During epic li-unbare the livespec primary was repeatedly observed regressing to core.bare=true (most recently 2026-05-28, .git/config mtime 22:25). git 2.51 worktree add/remove/prune does NOT flip core.bare (empirical probe on a throwaway repo, AND confirmed: a livespec worktree add+remove during the sweep left core.bare unset). No committed recipe (justfile/bootstrap/CI) sets it. Suspected trigger: a stale-branch 'just bootstrap' (pre-Phase-3a recipe that still ran 'git config core.bare true') invoked from one of the leftover agent worktrees, or a manual set. The primary-checkout-commit-refuse-hook-installed doctor invariant catches core.bare=true as a safety net (contracts.md:192), so it is self-healing on the next doctor run, but the trigger should be root-caused. Investigation: forensic on what wrote .git/config at the regression time; audit leftover worktrees on pre-Phase-3a branches for stale bootstrap recipes. Related: the stale-worktree-hygiene follow-up.
reason: completed — root cause pinned: git injects GIT_DIR=<worktree-gitdir> into the hook env when a commit hook fires inside a worktree; the commit-refuse wrapper delegated to lefthook WITHOUT clearing it, so lefthook misread the repo as bare and wrote core.bare=true into the shared .git/config. NOT a stale bootstrap recipe and NOT plain git — proven by A/B (same lefthook command WITH worktree GIT_DIR flips core.bare; WITHOUT stays unset; an unset-prefixed wrapper leaves it unset on a real commit). Fix landed via li-corebare-fix-wi1 (wrapper unsets GIT_DIR GIT_INDEX_FILE GIT_WORK_TREE GIT_PREFIX before lefthook, both wrapper files in sync, paired test). Follow-ups li-corebare-fix-wi2 (tighten fingerprint) + li-corebare-fix-wi3 (re-bootstrap the other three primaries) remain open.
title: Root-cause the core.bare transient re-set on the livespec primary (post-li-unbare)

Tighten the fingerprint to ALSO require the `--no-auto-install` flag and the `unset GIT_DIR ...` line, so a stale or partially-reverted wrapper that drops either guard is caught by the doctor static check rather than silently regressing the core.bare flip / auto-sync clobber. A proactive fingerprint-tightening guard was deemed not worth the 4-repo CI-stub coordination.

title: Eliminate core.bare flip + clobber + detection gap
Make red-green-replay protocol ambient + self-explaining
description: The red-green-replay OPERATIONAL commit sequence lives only in the hook source (livespec_dev_tooling/checks/red_green_replay.py + _red_green_replay_modes.py). No agent-readable place spells out the 2-step sequence, so every fresh sub-agent reverse-engineers it from source (~150K tokens observed). Make it ambient (repo + template CLAUDE.md, wi1) and self-explaining (hook reject messages, wi2).

title: Ambient Red-Green-Replay doc in repo-root + impl-plugin template CLAUDE.md
description: Add a crisp 'Red-Green-Replay commit protocol' section to repo-root CLAUDE.md AND the impl-plugin template root CLAUDE.md (so siblings inherit). 2-step: (1) Red commit = stage the test file ALONE, commit with fix:/feat: subject; hook re-runs pytest on staged state, the new test MUST fail meaningfully (assertion, NOT ImportError/collection error); records TDD-Red-* trailers. (2) Green amend = stage impl, git commit --amend; hook sees Red trailers + staged impl, re-runs test (now passing), records TDD-Green-* trailers. Final SINGLE commit carries both files + both trailer sets; test bytes identical across the pair. Non-impl-.py changesets exempt (use chore(...)/docs(...)). Doc-only.

Codify dispatch knowledge into dedicated Claude agent definitions (NEEDS DESIGN)
description: ROOT-CAUSE successor to li-rgr-docs. The Layer-3 orchestrator dispatches general-purpose agents with long hand-written briefs that re-paste the red-green-replay protocol + disciplines every time — the source of the ~150K-token reverse-engineering tax and the PR4 thrashing (coverage<->checksum-lock interplay, uv-run-vs-mise-exec, aggregate/working-tree-at-Red, pyright-noise confusion). Adopt Claude Code custom agent definitions (.claude/agents/<name>.md) as the dispatch executor: a livespec-implementer agent whose system prompt codifies the standing contract ONCE (red-green-replay 2-step incl the coverage<->checksum-lock trap + new-module stub technique; worktree/mise-exec/no---no-verify; release-please + pin-bump mechanics; per-repo gating differences); maybe a lighter livespec-doc agent; orchestrate dispatch table selects agentType so briefs shrink to 'implement work-item X in repo Y'. DESIGN DISCIPLINES: (1) do NOT create a 5th source of truth — the agent prompt POINTS AT the canonical AGENTS.md (shipped by li-rgr-docs) + hook reject messages, carrying only the must-not-violate distillation; (2) per-repo variation is the hard part (conditionals vs read-the-repo-AGENTS.md); (3) composes with, does not replace, the impl-plugin implement skill. FURTHER OPTION: mechanize the ritual (a just tdd-commit helper performing the Red-commit then Green-amend dance) so the error-prone 2-step is not hand-orchestrated. NEEDS DESIGN — not yet scoped into sub-tasks/waves; flesh out before implementing.

title: Close the pyright gating gap in dev-tooling + runtime (NEEDS DESIGN)
description: dev-tooling has 681 pyright errors and pyright (check-types) is gated NOWHERE: not in the just check aggregate (it is a tool-backed check, explicitly not a canonical-aggregate target) and not in the CI matrix (the Python matrix runs only check-lint/check-format/check-coverage; the matrix comment aspirationally lists types but the list omits it). runtime has the SAME omission. livespec + impl-plaintext DO gate check-types and report 0. Of the 681: ~660 (~97%) are the vendored-structlog stub-resolution cascade (44 reportMissingImports trigger -> 497 reportUnknownMemberType + 76 unknown-variable + 23/20 unknown param/arg) — the SAME pattern the livespec pyright epic li-xxjopf already solved via extraPaths + stubPath + per-vendor stubs; ~21 are genuine strict findings (reportUnusedCallResult, reportArgumentType, reportUnusedFunction, etc.). FIX DIRECTION: (1) port the proven li-xxjopf cascade fix (extraPaths+stubPath+per-vendor stubs, memory project_pyright_cascade_fix) to dev-tooling + runtime; (2) clean the residual ~21 genuine findings; (3) wire check-types into the just check aggregate AND the CI matrix in both repos; (4) add a meta-check guarding tool-backed-check (lint/format/types/coverage) completeness in the CI matrix so a tool-backed check can never again silently drop (aggregate-completeness only guards CANONICAL checks). NEEDS DESIGN/SCOPING — not yet scoped into sub-tasks; flesh out before implementing.

The `/livespec-orchestrate` Layer 3 driver's session-state surfacing step (per `.claude/skills/livespec-orchestrate/SKILL.md`) uses a bare `gh run list --branch master --limit 1` query to check master CI state per family repo. This misses red CI when a non-CI workflow has run more recently than the last CI failure. The livespec family runs several frequent non-CI workflows ("Auto-update behind PRs" on cron, "Pin freshness sweep", "Bump pin from sibling dispatch" on release events, "Release Please"); any of those can mask a red CI workflow run.

Observed real consequence: the 2026-05-28 li-unbare orchestration session missed a 13-hour-old livespec master CI red (the `doctor-wiring-completeness-cross-repo` check failing in CI from a pre-existing infrastructure gap). The missed signal triggered an unnecessary Phase 3a sub-agent halt mid-iteration and an architectural detour. Per [[master-red-is-not-deferral]] this was supposed to be the first halt.

Fix options:
- Option A (minimal): Update orchestrator SKILL.md prose to mandate `--workflow CI` when querying CI state. Doc-only; relies on agent compliance.
- Option B (structural): Add a thin-transport helper script under `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/` or `.claude/skills/livespec-orchestrate/scripts/` that the orchestrator calls to do the session-state survey correctly. Encodes the right query mechanically.
- Option C (best): Have the orchestrator call dev-tooling's existing `master_ci_green.py` check directly for each family repo (with the appropriate repo path arg) — it already encodes the right query semantics (`--workflow CI`). Reuse beats reimplementation.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Orchestrator's session-state survey CANNOT miss a red CI workflow run because a non-CI workflow ran more recently — verified by test or manual repro against current master.
2. Applied across all 4 family repos (or designed to scale to future siblings).
3. Mistake-pattern mechanically prevented, not just documented.

Related memory: [[master-ci-query-must-filter-workflow]]
reason: Implemented via PR #339 (commit b450f16, merged to master). Orchestrator SKILL.md Step 1 now runs dev-tooling's master_ci_green check (which scopes the query to --workflow CI) from inside each of the 4 family repos, so a more-recent non-CI workflow can no longer mask a red CI run. Option C (reuse dev-tooling's existing check) over reimplementation. Acceptance 1-3 satisfied; the stale 'NOT included here' NOTE in the aggregation section was reconciled to point at the landed check.
title: orchestrator session-state survey misses red CI when non-CI workflow ran more recently
Author .claude/agents/livespec-implementer.md + wire orchestrate dispatch table
description: Author the unified `livespec-implementer` custom subagent definition at `/data/projects/livespec/.claude/agents/livespec-implementer.md` per the SETTLED design in tmp/prompt.md Epic A. Frontmatter: name=livespec-implementer; one-line description = dispatch executor for the livespec family that creates its own secondary worktree, does the work, commits per the target repo's rules, opens+arms a PR, reports the PR number; model: inherit; permissionMode: inherit; NO `tools` field (inherit all); NO `isolation` field (self-manages a secondary worktree). System-prompt body points AT AGENTS.md (does NOT duplicate the RGR protocol): Step 0 read <worktree>/AGENTS.md; worktree creation via `git -C /data/projects/<repo> worktree add <repo>/.claude/worktrees/<slug> -b <branch> origin/master` with absolute paths + git -C (cd does not persist); commit style by changeset (RGR 2-step for product .py, single chore/docs/chore(spec) otherwise; always `mise exec -- git`; never --no-verify / core.bare true / git checkout master; on hook reject READ the message and fix or HALT); for tracked work-items invoke `/livespec-impl-plaintext:implement <id>` via Skill; PR+handoff via `gh pr create` then `gh pr merge --auto --rebase --delete-branch`, report the PR number (READ it from gh pr create output, do not guess) + 'auto-merge armed' + worktree/branch, then exit without polling or self-cleaning. ALSO wire the orchestrate SKILL.md §'Dispatch table' + §'Steps'->Dispatch to select `agentType: livespec-implementer` with a SHORT brief plus a one-line binding-rules handoff naming the exact path '<worktree>/AGENTS.md — read first'. Markdown-only (chore/docs); if a .py smoke test asserting the agent def parses / SKILL.md references it is added, that test rides RGR.

title: Family-wide AGENTS.md Red-Green-Replay accuracy refinement (Red-gate wording + new-module stub technique)
description: In AGENTS.md §'Red-Green-Replay commit protocol' across ALL 4 repos + the impl-plugin template: (a) correct the Red-gate wording — the hook requires the staged test to FAIL on pytest non-zero; the 'assertion, NOT ImportError/collection-error' phrasing is guidance to avoid FALSE reds, not a literal hook requirement; (b) document the new-module stub technique (when the impl module doesn't exist yet, create it as a STUB so Red fails on an assertion, not an ImportError/collection error). Doc-only; separate PR per repo (template lives in livespec). While here, fix livespec AGENTS.md ~L57 which still says dispatch uses 'worktree isolation' — correct to the self-managed-secondary model (one-obvious-fix). Inherited from li-rgr-docs' deferral.

Deliberately NOT in Epic A scope — the agent knowing the ritual cold is the 80% win; this is the safety-net follow-up. Sequenced AFTER Epic A.

title: livespec CI guard: validate work-items.jsonl against the impl-plaintext store schema
description: A malformed work-items.jsonl merged green (PRs #341/#342 filed li-dispatch-agents-wi1/wi2 + li-tddcommit WITHOUT the required `superseded_by` key), making the entire store unreadable by list_work_items/materialize_work_items (SchemaViolationError at the first bad record) — the orchestrator's own session-survey tool + the impl-plaintext `next` ranker both broke. Root cause: livespec CI does NOT validate work-items.jsonl against the impl-plaintext store schema (_WORK_ITEM_REQUIRED_KEYS, 15 required keys). Repaired ad hoc in PR #345. FIX: add a livespec CI/just-check guard that runs the impl-plaintext schema validation (read_work_items / materialize_work_items, or an equivalent required-keys check) over livespec's work-items.jsonl, so a record missing a required key (or otherwise schema-invalid) can never merge green again. Consider whether this belongs as a shared dev-tooling check (so all family repos that carry a work-items.jsonl get it) vs a livespec-local check; the impl-plaintext store module is the schema source of truth. NEEDS DESIGN on placement (shared dev-tooling check importing the impl-plaintext schema, vs a thin livespec check shelling the impl-plaintext wrapper in --filter all mode and asserting exit 0).

description:
Surfaced by check-no-stale-revise-branches once wired into livespec's canonical aggregate (li-livwir, epic li-univck self-host).

## Background

The newly-wired canonical check check-no-stale-revise-branches refuses to pass while local branch refs prefixed `spec/*` are ahead of origin/master. Inspection found 4 stale local-ref branches whose tip commits have all been cherry-picked into origin/master under different SHAs (the canonical livespec revise flow lands a cherry-pick of the spec/* tip, not a merge):

- spec/recast-layer-3-livespec-only (1 commit ahead; subject matches master e04afb3)
- spec/recast-layer-3-revise (4 commits ahead; tip subject matches master cfef952)
- spec/rename-loop-to-orchestrate (3 commits ahead; tip subject matches master 603c3ff)
- spec/revise-wiring-completeness (1 commit ahead; tip subject matches master 413680e)

All 4 are orphan refs whose work has already landed; safe to delete.

## Scope

1. Verify each tip via `git log --grep` on subject against origin/master to confirm cherry-pick landing.
2. Delete each local branch ref via `git branch -D <name>`.
3. Re-run `just check-no-stale-revise-branches` to confirm green.

## Acceptance

- check-no-stale-revise-branches exits 0 from a fresh clone or from the primary checkout.
- No spec/* branches remain locally that aren't actively in use by an open worktree.

## Notes

li-livwir's PR merges with check-no-stale-revise-branches in the failed list (1 of 2 known failures, the other being a comment-no-historical-refs typo fixed in-line). The CI matrix entry for check-no-stale-revise-branches may need to gate appropriately, or the check may need to be made non-CI (it operates on local branch refs which are not meaningfully populated in CI clones).
reason: All 4 stale spec/* branches (spec/recast-layer-3-livespec-only, spec/recast-layer-3-revise, spec/rename-loop-to-orchestrate, spec/revise-wiring-completeness) confirmed deleted; no local spec/* refs remain (git for-each-ref refs/heads/spec/ empty); check-no-stale-revise-branches green (just check-no-stale-revise-branches exits 0). Verified 2026-05-30.
title: Delete stale spec/* branches surfaced by check-no-stale-revise-branches

reason: RE-OPENED 2026-05-30: PR #354 closed this on a false premise — the tool-backed-check completeness meta-check does NOT exist on dev-tooling master (no tool_backed_check_completeness.py, not a recipe, not in just check aggregate; just check = 39 targets). GENUINE DESIGN FORK before implementing: dev-tooling's `just check` aggregate does NOT literally contain check-lint/check-format/check-coverage (they run transitively; only check-types + check-per-file-coverage are literal members), so a strict 'tool-backed check must be a literal member of BOTH just check AND CI matrix' meta-check cannot self-pass on dev-tooling. Options: (a) scope the meta-check to check-types only (the actual li-pyright-gate gap); (b) add lint/format/coverage as literal aggregate members (behavior change to local just check); (c) define 'present in just check' as transitive-reachability not literal membership (weaker guarantee). Needs human decision before impl. Prior impl WIP existed in a stale worktree (now reaped).

description: The /livespec:revise wrapper's --post-step-doctor phase invokes bin/doctor_static.py as a subprocess via bare `sys.executable` (plain python3). doctor_static imports livespec.doctor.static.wiring_completeness_cross_repo, which imports `livespec_dev_tooling.canonical_checks`; `livespec_dev_tooling` is not importable under bare python3 in the source-repo dogfood flow -> ModuleNotFoundError -> doctor_static produces EMPTY stdout -> revise.py raises PreconditionError 'post-step doctor stdout malformed JSON: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)' and exits 3. OBSERVED during the v097 revise (livespec PR #358, accepting cli-end-to-end-harness-contract). IMPORTANT: the revise file-shaping itself SUCCEEDED (v097 cut, PC moved, revision paired, contracts.md updated); only the post-step doctor GATE is non-functional. So the unresolved-spec-commitment invariant (and the rest of the static registry) is silently bypassed at revise time -- a broken safety gate. WORKAROUND used this session: run doctor_static via the primary's `uv run` env (which has livespec_dev_tooling), where it passes with 0 failures. PROPOSED FIX: revise.py must invoke doctor_static through a dependency-resolved interpreter (e.g. `uv run`, or ensure livespec_dev_tooling is importable on the subprocess path -- vendor it / set PYTHONPATH). RELATED: same dependency-resolution class as li-l6asrr / li-tvi7lm (doctor wrapper importing sibling packages absent from the bundle). Likely also affects the INSTALLED-plugin revise flow if livespec_dev_tooling is not vendored in the livespec bundle -- which is exactly the install-shape gap epic li-e2ecli's CLI e2e harness is designed to catch.
title: revise --post-step-doctor crashes: doctor_static.py shelled via bare python3 cannot import livespec_dev_tooling

description: The reaper (livespec dev-tooling/reap_stale_worktrees.py) reaps a dispatched agent's NEVER-PUSHED in-progress worktree. Its case-b 'branch absent from remote' detection fires for branches that never had an upstream tracking ref (never pushed), treating them identically to branches that WERE pushed then merged/deleted. So when the Layer-3 orchestrator runs `just reap-stale-worktrees <repo>` while a livespec-implementer is mid-dispatch (worktree created, branch local-only, work not yet committed/pushed), the reaper deletes the worktree AND the branch out from under the live agent. OBSERVED this session: reaped worktrees/li-revise-doctor-bug while its filing agent (li-rvdctr) was mid-task -- the worktrees/ dir went empty and the branch vanished before the agent committed. The agent was defensive (re-fetched, recreated the worktree off the new origin/master tip, re-validated, proceeded with no loss), but a less-defensive agent would lose committed-but-unpushed work. TWO FIXES: (1) REAPER: case-b should fire ONLY for branches that HAVE (or had) a remote-tracking ref now gone -- distinguish 'never pushed' (in-progress; KEEP) from 'pushed then remote-gone/merged' (orphan; reap). A clean worktree on a local-only branch with no upstream is in-progress work, not an orphan. (2) ORCHESTRATOR (mitigation, separate): never run the reaper while an agent is actively dispatched into that repo; reap only at session start / after a confirmed merge / loop end. RELATED: epic li-wtreap (worktree hygiene).
title: reap_stale_worktrees reaps never-pushed in-progress worktrees (no-upstream branch treated as remote-gone), racing active dispatch

description: DEFERRED from li-e2ecpx (Wave 12, epic li-e2ecli). impl-plaintext's product code has NEVER been subject to the shared shape/ROP checks: at dev-tooling v0.6.0 those checks hardcoded `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec`, which does not exist in impl-plaintext, so they walked an empty tree and VACUOUSLY passed. v0.8.0 made the checks config-driven; declaring `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling].source_trees` (asybpo criterion 4, li-asybpo) so they run against impl-plaintext's own tree surfaces 32 previously-masked findings: 14 `all_declared` (missing `__all__`, mechanical); 10 `keyword_only_args` (8 mechanical, plus `next.py:_sort_key` which is passed to `sort(key=...)` -> Python calls it POSITIONALLY so it MUST stay positional, but the check has no sort/sorted-key carve-out); 8 `no_except_outside_io` (impl-plaintext has no `io/` directory; its try/except is woven into store.py + the commands/* supervisors, and the `io_trees` role is strictly DIRECTORY-based). TWO architectural sub-decisions: (1) DEV-TOOLING FIX (clear): `keyword_only_args` MUST exempt sort/sorted-key callables (positional is required by Python's sort API) -- a small livespec-dev-tooling change. (2) IO-LAYER FORK (genuine, USER DECISION): reconcile the woven I/O either by (A) refactoring impl-plaintext into an `io/`-layered package (matches livespec-core's I/O-boundary discipline; architecturally consistent; larger), OR (B) extending the dev-tooling checks to express file-level io roles / supervisor-catch exemptions (smaller; dilutes the package-boundary guardrail). Deliberately NOT declared during Wave 12 so the harness wiring (li-e2ecpx) could land green; status quo = vacuous pass, NO regression. RELATED: li-asybpo (criterion 4 was scoped to dev-tooling only), li-e2ecpx, li-e2eclv. Likely warrants splitting into the dev-tooling sort-key fix + the io-layer decision + the mechanical __all__/kwonly cleanup.
title: impl-plaintext: adopt v0.8.0 config-driven shared checks (source_trees) — 32 masked findings + io-layer architecture decision
