description: A `just tdd-commit` helper mechanizing the Red-commit-then-Green-amend ritual (stage test -> commit -> stage impl -> amend) so the error-prone 2-step isn't hand-orchestrated. dev-tooling recipe + helper script + tests + release + 4-repo adoption. Deliberately NOT in Epic A scope — the agent knowing the ritual cold is the 80% win; this is the safety-net follow-up. Sequenced AFTER Epic A.

## Goal

Refactor every shared check in livespec_dev_tooling/checks/ to read its source-tree paths from configuration rather than hardcoded constants. This unlocks (a) restoring fail_under=100 on coverage by un-skipping the three tests skipped in G.4's migration commit, (b) livespec-dev-tooling self-application against its own tree, and (c) livespec-impl-* consumers running the checks against their own non-livespec-core layouts.

## Background

Phase G.4 of epic li-fgqgnk (commit c7d9603 in livespec-dev-tooling) migrated 34 shared checks AS-IS. Their hardcoded constants like `_LIVESPEC_TREE = Path('.claude-plugin') / 'scripts' / 'livespec'` still match livespec-core's layout exactly, so livespec-core (Phase G.6 consumer) works immediately. But other consumers (livespec-impl-plaintext at G.7, livespec-dev-tooling itself for self-application) get silent-no-op behavior from any check whose default tree doesn't exist in their layout.

Three tests are explicitly skipped pending this work:

- test_branch_protection_alignment::test_real_repo_passes — the check hardcodes `_REPO_OWNER_REPO = 'thewoolleyman/livespec'`.
- test_check_coverage_incremental::test_resolve_mirror_test_for_dev_tooling_checks_pair — the function maps livespec-core's sourceâtest mirror schema.
- test_check_coverage_incremental::test_main_passes_against_fully_covered_real_repo_pair — invokes the check against `dev-tooling/checks/all_declared.py` which doesn't exist in livespec-dev-tooling's layout.

## Design sketch

Introduce a config-file convention: each consumer's pyproject.toml (or, fallback, an env var like LIVESPEC_DEV_TOOLING_SOURCE_TREES with colon-separated paths) declares the source trees the checks walk. Each check reads the config at startup; falls back to its historical livespec-core defaults if no config is present. `branch_protection_alignment.py` reads the repo identifier from `git remote get-url origin` (parsing the github.com URL) rather than the hardcoded constant.

## Acceptance

1. All 34 shared checks accept a source-tree config and fall back to livespec-core defaults when none is provided.
2. livespec-dev-tooling's pyproject.toml declares a `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling].source_trees = ["livespec_dev_tooling"]` entry and `just check` runs every check against the library's own tree (self-application per SPECIFICATION/constraints.md §"Self-application").
3. The three skipped tests are restored (`@pytest.mark.skip` removed); coverage gate restored to `fail_under = 100`.
4. livespec-impl-plaintext's pyproject.toml (Phase G.7) similarly declares its own source-trees; its `just check` exercises the shared checks against its own tree.
5. livespec-core's pyproject.toml (Phase G.6) either declares source-trees explicitly OR relies on the historical defaults; either way, livespec-core's `just check` stays bit-identical to the pre-G.6 baseline.

## Dependency

Depends on G.7 (li-nyxtnb) completion. Until G.7 lands, the consumers are not yet rewired and the refactor has no validation surface beyond livespec-dev-tooling itself.
reason: Implemented via livespec-dev-tooling PR #71 (merged; v0.7.0): config-driven source-tree loader + 17 checks refactored + fail_under=100 restored + 3 skipped tests un-skipped; livespec-core bit-identical. Criterion 4 (impl-plaintext source_trees) deferred to li-pxsrc.
title: Path-portability refactor for livespec-dev-tooling checks (restore 100% coverage + self-application + cross-consumer use)

reason: Implemented via livespec-dev-tooling PR #72 (merged; v0.7.0): tool-backed-check-completeness meta-check (LITERAL-membership design, settled with user) + lint/format/coverage wired as literal `just check` members (coverage-reuse, no double run).
title: dev-tooling: add tool-backed-check completeness meta-check (model on aggregate-completeness) + release dev-tooling + bump consumer pins
Close the pyright gating gap in dev-tooling + runtime (NEEDS DESIGN)
description: dev-tooling has 681 pyright errors and pyright (check-types) is gated NOWHERE: not in the just check aggregate (it is a tool-backed check, explicitly not a canonical-aggregate target) and not in the CI matrix (the Python matrix runs only check-lint/check-format/check-coverage; the matrix comment aspirationally lists types but the list omits it). runtime has the SAME omission. livespec + impl-plaintext DO gate check-types and report 0. Of the 681: ~660 (~97%) are the vendored-structlog stub-resolution cascade (44 reportMissingImports trigger -> 497 reportUnknownMemberType + 76 unknown-variable + 23/20 unknown param/arg) — the SAME pattern the livespec pyright epic li-xxjopf already solved via extraPaths + stubPath + per-vendor stubs; ~21 are genuine strict findings (reportUnusedCallResult, reportArgumentType, reportUnusedFunction, etc.). FIX DIRECTION: (1) port the proven li-xxjopf cascade fix (extraPaths+stubPath+per-vendor stubs, memory project_pyright_cascade_fix) to dev-tooling + runtime; (2) clean the residual ~21 genuine findings; (3) wire check-types into the just check aggregate AND the CI matrix in both repos; (4) add a meta-check guarding tool-backed-check (lint/format/types/coverage) completeness in the CI matrix so a tool-backed check can never again silently drop (aggregate-completeness only guards CANONICAL checks). NEEDS DESIGN/SCOPING — not yet scoped into sub-tasks; flesh out before implementing.
reason: Epic complete: wi1 (dev-tooling #68), wi2 (runtime #25), wi3 (dev-tooling #72) all merged. dev-tooling + runtime gate check-types at 0; the meta-check guards tool-backed-check completeness in both `just check` and CI.

description:
## Scope

Propose-change against `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` introducing a new section (working title: `## CLI end-to-end harness contract`) that mandates a top-of-pyramid user-surface end-to-end test for every plugin in the livespec family.

## Why this contract exists

Today's `## E2E harness contract` (lines ~674-754) drives wrapper scripts as Python subprocesses anchored at `_REPO_ROOT/.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/`. That's a *wrapper-surface* test, not a *user-surface* test. It does not exercise the `claude` CLI binary, plugin marketplace install, plugin loading, or slash-command dispatch as a real end user does. Install-shape bugs (missing vendored runtime, broken SKILL.md invocation paths, etc.) are invisible to it.

## Normative requirements the new contract MUST encode

1. **Sole entry point is the `claude` CLI binary.** Setup pre-populates a tmp `HOME` with `~/.claude/settings.json` declaring the marketplace + enabled plugin (or runs `claude -p "/plugin install ..."` as the first step). Workflow steps are `claude -p` subprocess invocations issuing slash commands (`--continue`/`--resume <id>` for multi-turn). No reach-around to wrapper Python files. No knowledge of cache layout. No claude-agent-sdk programmatic surface; the SDK is the wrapper-chain tier, not this tier.
2. **Pluggable implementation.** The harness drives an upstream `livespec` plugin in lockstep with one *impl* plugin (today `livespec-impl-plaintext`; tomorrow alternate implementations). The impl-plugin id is a parameter to the harness. The spec-side skill set is fixed; the impl-side skill set is whatever the installed impl plugin exposes.
3. **Structural skill discovery.** Skill enumeration walks `<installed-plugin>/skills/*/SKILL.md` in each plugin's installed location. Plugin slash-command prefix is read from `plugin.json`'s `name` field. No parallel manifest file; the Claude Code plugin directory structure is the canonical source of truth.
4. **Per-skill fixtures as a parallel filesystem convention.** A test fixtures directory (suggested layout: `<consumer-repo>/tests/e2e-cli/fixtures/<skill>/`) holds a `prompt.md` (text piped to `claude -p`) and an `expected_files.txt` (paths that must exist after) per skill. Discovery walks the same way: directory == fixture exists.
5. **Time-bomb coverage gate (fail-closed).** The harness MUST assert `discovered_skills - fixtured_skills == empty_set` and fail the test run otherwise. New skill added to either plugin trips the gate until either (a) a fixture directory is added or (b) the skill is explicitly listed in an `EXEMPT_SKILLS` table in the consumer repo with a written justification.
6. **Single canonical implementation in `livespec-dev-tooling`.** The harness itself (driver, fixtures loader, discovery, coverage gate) ships from `livespec-dev-tooling` and is consumed by every plugin repo via the existing pin-bump dependency flow. Each consumer repo wires the imported test function into its own pytest collection.
7. **Consumer obligations.** Each plugin claiming livespec-family membership MUST: (a) be installable solely via the `claude` CLI plugin-install surface; (b) ship a `tests/e2e-cli/` directory with per-skill fixtures; (c) pass the imported harness against itself (and, where applicable, against the upstream `livespec` plugin paired in lockstep).

## Relationship to the existing `## E2E harness contract`

The existing wrapper-chain harness STAYS. It is the second-tier integration test (faster, deterministic, mock-LLM). The new contract adds a higher tier whose only entry point is the user-visible CLI. Both tiers coexist in CI; the new tier replaces neither.

## Acceptance

- New `## CLI end-to-end harness contract` section accepted into `contracts.md` via the normal propose-change → revise flow.
- Section is registered in `tests/heading-coverage.json`.
- Spec-side `## E2E harness contract` is amended to clarify it is the wrapper-chain tier (sibling to, not superset of, the new CLI tier).
reason: Implemented via livespec PR #358 (merged; revise v097): `## CLI end-to-end harness contract` (7 requirements) added to contracts.md; existing E2E harness contract amended to 'wrapper-chain tier'; heading registered.
title: Spec contract: top-of-pyramid CLI-driven e2e harness contract (epic li-e2ecli Phase 1)

description:
## Scope

Propose-change against `livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" adding a sub-rule that strengthens the existing heading-coverage invariant for `scenarios.md` entries specifically: each scenario's mapped test MUST live at the **middle (integration) or top (e2e / CLI) tier** of the test pyramid, not at the unit tier.

## Current state

`constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" (lines 219-235) mandates that every `##` heading in a template-declared NLSpec file have an entry in `tests/heading-coverage.json` mapping to a pytest node id. The rule is uniform across spec files — a unit test at `tests/livespec/commands/test_seed.py::...` satisfies the gate for a `## Scenario: ...` heading just as it does for a `## Specification model` heading.

But scenarios are categorically different: scenarios.md describes user-observable behavior. A scenario covered only by a unit test isn't really covered — the unit test verifies internal shape, not the user-observable round trip the scenario describes. Today's gate accepts that substitution silently.

## Proposed amendment

Add a sub-rule to §"Heading taxonomy" along these lines:

> For entries whose `spec_file` is `scenarios.md`, the mapped `test` node id MUST > resolve to a test in the integration tier or above. Operationally: the node-id > path component MUST begin with one of the integration-tier prefixes (e.g., > `tests.e2e.…`, `tests.integration.…`, `tests.prompts.…`, …) enumerated in the > per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist; OR the resolved > test function MUST carry an explicit `pytest.mark.integration` (or stronger) > marker. `TODO` entries are still allowed during transition but MUST carry a > reason explicitly acknowledging the tier requirement.

The check `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.heading_coverage` MUST be extended to enforce this sub-rule, with a new diagnostic class (e.g., `scenario heading mapped to unit-tier test`).

## Why a per-repo allowlist

Different repos place integration tests in different directories (`tests/e2e/`, `tests/integration/`, `tests/consumer/`, `tests/prompts/`). The rule should be structural (a prefix list) rather than hard-coded so each repo can declare its own convention without amending the shared check.

## Out of scope

- Re-tier-ing any existing `scenarios.md` entries that currently point at unit-tier tests. That's per-repo cleanup work (see Phases 2-3 of this epic for the library repos; livespec itself is already mostly-compliant per the audit in the epic body).
- Forcing the same rule on `contracts.md`, `constraints.md`, or `spec.md` headings. Those legitimately may be exercised by unit-tier tests; only scenarios are user-observable-by-definition.

## Acceptance

- New sub-rule accepted into `constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" via propose-change → revise.
- `heading_coverage` check extended to enforce the scenario-tier rule, with the per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist mechanism.
- Dev-tooling release cut at the matching version; downstream repos can bump-pin to pick up the strengthened check.
- livespec's own `scenarios.md` entries audited: any currently pointing at unit-tier tests are re-pointed at e2e equivalents or moved to TODO with a tier-rationale reason.
reason: Implemented via livespec PR #360 (merged; revise v098): constraints.md §Heading taxonomy sub-rule requiring scenarios.md entries map to integration-tier-or-above tests (per-repo scenario_tiers allowlist). The heading_coverage CHECK EXTENSION + entry re-tiering remain in li-scetier Phases 2-3 / Wave 14.
title: Strengthen livespec heading-coverage to require middle/top-of-pyramid tests for scenarios.md (epic li-scetier Phase 1)

description:
## Scope

Implement the harness defined by the Phase 1 contract amendment in the shared `livespec-dev-tooling` package, cut a release, and prepare consumers to bump-pin.

## What ships

1. **Driver:** a pytest test function (or pytest plugin entry point) consumers import via `from livespec_dev_tooling.testing.cli_e2e import test_workflow_full_round_trip`. Takes a config object naming the impl-plugin id, the marketplace coordinates, and the fixtures directory. Sets `HOME=$tmp`, writes `~/.claude/settings.json` declaring the marketplace + enabled plugin, optionally runs the install slash command, then walks discovered skills through `claude -p` invocations (with `--continue`/`--resume` for multi-turn).
2. **Discovery helper:** walks `<plugin_install_dir>/skills/*/SKILL.md`, reads `plugin.json` for the slash-command prefix, returns `{plugin_id: [skill_names]}`. No manifest file.
3. **Coverage gate:** asserts `discovered_skills - fixtured_skills - exempt_skills == set()`; fails with the missing-skill list when not.
4. **Step orchestrator:** for each discovered skill, looks up `<fixtures>/<skill>/{prompt.md,expected_files.txt}`, runs the prompt via `claude -p`, asserts files exist + exit code 0. Handles multi-turn via session resume.
5. **Wireable to existing `LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS` selector** so the new tier slots into the family-wide convention without inventing a new env-var dialect.

## Out of scope

- Consumer-side integration (Phase 3 + 4).
- Real-tier LLM-driven workflows. The new harness is structural — `claude -p` invocations with canned prompts; the LLM-driven equivalent can be a future extension.

## Acceptance

- New release of `livespec-dev-tooling` (v0.5.0 or whatever the next major slot is) publishing the harness surface.
- Self-test inside `livespec-dev-tooling` exercises the harness against a tiny single-skill fixture-plugin to prove the discovery + coverage-gate + driver loop work in isolation.
- API documented in `livespec-dev-tooling`'s README + the consumer-facing import path is stable.
reason: Implemented via livespec-dev-tooling PR #73 (merged; v0.8.0): CLI-e2e harness (driver+discovery+fixtures+time-bomb gate+orchestrator+importable entry point); mock tier does real install+discovery.
title: Implement CLI e2e harness in livespec-dev-tooling + release (epic li-e2ecli Phase 2)

description:
## Scope

Bump-pin `livespec` to the new `livespec-dev-tooling` release (Phase 2), add a CI job that runs the harness parametrized across every known impl plugin.

## What changes in this repo

1. Bump-pin `livespec-dev-tooling` in `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`.
2. Add `tests/e2e-cli/` with per-skill fixtures for every `/livespec:*` skill (seed, propose-change, critique, revise, doctor, prune-history, help, next, ...). Fixtures cover the spec-side happy path.
3. Add a tiny registry of known impl plugins (today: just impl-plaintext) — a list of `(plugin_id, marketplace_url)` tuples consumed by a parametrized fixture.
4. Add a CI workflow (or extend an existing one) invoking the harness for each (spec, impl) pair. Headless `claude -p` only; no tmux. Job installs `claude` CLI via `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` (same step `e2e-real.yml` already uses).
5. Heading-coverage entry for the new contract section if not already added in Phase 1.
6. Confirm the time-bomb gate fires correctly when a deliberate fixture is removed (red baseline test).

## Acceptance

- New CI job (e.g. `e2e-cli.yml`) green on master.
- Adding a new skill to `livespec` without a fixture fails the new job loudly.
- `just check` (or whatever local equivalent) runs the new tier in mock-cheap form for local development.
reason: Implemented via livespec PR #362 (merged): tests/e2e-cli/ fixtures for all 8 /livespec:* skills + imported harness (mock tier) + CI + time-bomb gate; pin v0.8.0 + meta-check wiring. 927 tests, 100% cov.
title: Wire CLI e2e harness into livespec repo CI (epic li-e2ecli Phase 3)

description:
## Scope

Bump-pin `livespec-impl-plaintext` to the new `livespec-dev-tooling` release, wire the harness into its CI, surface and resolve the install-shape bugs the harness will immediately discover.

## What changes in this repo

1. Bump-pin `livespec-dev-tooling` in `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`.
2. Add `tests/e2e-cli/` with per-skill fixtures for every `/livespec-impl-plaintext:*` skill — every authored heavyweight skill and every thin-transport surface listed in `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md`.
3. Add a CI workflow invoking the imported harness against the impl-plaintext plugin (paired with upstream `livespec`).
4. Expect the first harness run to fail. The expected failure mode is the union of the two impl-plaintext bugs already filed (see §Side-effect closures below). Each underlying defect MUST be fixed before this sub-task can close.
5. Heading-coverage entry mirroring upstream.

## Side-effect closures (impl-plaintext work-items.jsonl)

The following two P1 bugs already filed in `livespec-impl-plaintext/work-items.jsonl` are *expected* to be discovered by the new harness on first run and MUST be closed as part of completing this sub-task:

- **`li-k5dadf`** — Plugin bundle ships without `_vendor/livespec_runtime/`. Every cross-repo skill (next, list-work-items, capture-spec-drift, ...) fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'livespec_runtime'` when invoked from a fresh cache install. The spec mandates the vendor layout at `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Vendored dependencies in `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/`"; `_vendor/` has never been committed at any SHA. Fixing this is the precondition for any `claude -p` skill invocation to succeed against the cache install path.
- **`li-m4q4h5`** — Every SKILL.md quotes wrapper paths in the `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/<name>.py` form. Claude Code's installer flattens `.claude-plugin/scripts/` → `scripts/` in the cache. Cache also omits `pyproject.toml`/`uv.lock`, so `uv run` has no fallback. Fixing this is the precondition for any SKILL.md invocation block to be executable from the installed location.

Once both bugs are fixed and verified, the harness should go green end-to-end against the impl-plaintext plugin. The two bug records are then closed with audit pointers to the PR that lands this sub-task.

## Acceptance

- New CI job green on impl-plaintext master.
- `li-k5dadf` and `li-m4q4h5` records closed with resolution audit pointing here.
- A real `claude /plugin install ...` from a fresh tmp `HOME` followed by `claude -p "/livespec-impl-plaintext:next"` succeeds end-to-end.
reason: Implemented via livespec-impl-plaintext PR #63 (merged): tests/e2e-cli/ fixtures for all 10 /livespec-impl-plaintext:* skills + imported harness (mock tier) + CI; pin v0.8.0 + meta-check wiring. source_trees deferred to li-pxsrc; no new install-shape bugs.
title: Wire CLI e2e harness into livespec-impl-plaintext repo (epic li-e2ecli Phase 4)

description:
Coordinating epic to add a *user-surface* end-to-end integration test tier that drives the `claude` CLI binary itself — installing plugins via the CLI's plugin surface and exercising skills via slash commands from a foreign tmp project root.

## Motivation

The existing `## E2E harness contract` (contracts.md ~L674-754) drives wrapper scripts as Python subprocesses anchored at `_REPO_ROOT/.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/`. That is a wrapper-surface test, not a user-surface test. It does not validate that an end user — whose only interaction surface is `claude /plugin install ...` followed by typing slash commands — can install a livespec-family plugin and run its skills successfully.

Two P1 bugs filed against `livespec-impl-plaintext` on 2026-05-28 surface the gap concretely: the published plugin bundle is missing `_vendor/livespec_runtime/` (`li-k5dadf`), and every SKILL.md quotes a wrapper path that doesn't survive the installer's directory flattening (`li-m4q4h5`). Both bugs are invisible to every test in every livespec-family repo today, because every test runs from the dev tree where the bugs don't manifest. A single CLI-driven end-to-end test would have caught both immediately.

## Architecture (mechanics, not prose)

- **Driver:** headless `claude -p` invocations from pytest, multi-turn via `--continue`/`--resume <id>`. No tmux (terminal scraping is brittle and adds no signal). No claude-agent-sdk programmatic surface (that's a parallel surface; we want the CLI binary itself).
- **Pluggable impl:** spec-side `/livespec:*` skills are fixed; impl-side `/<impl-plugin>:*` skills are parametrized. The same imported test runs against any (livespec, impl-plugin) pair.
- **Structural discovery:** walk `<installed-plugin>/skills/*/SKILL.md`; read `plugin.json` for the slash-command prefix. No manifest file — the Claude Code plugin directory layout IS the contract.
- **Time-bomb coverage gate:** harness fails closed if any discovered skill lacks a fixture directory. New skill → next CI run blows up until either a fixture lands or the skill is explicitly exempted with written justification.
- **Single canonical implementation:** ships from `livespec-dev-tooling`; every consumer repo imports it via the existing pin-bump dependency flow.

## Phases

- **Phase 1 — Spec contract.** `li-e2ecspec`: propose-change against `livespec` `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` adding the new `## CLI end-to-end harness contract` section. Mandates the entry-point discipline, pluggable-impl shape, structural discovery, fixture convention, and time-bomb coverage gate. Distinct from (and coexists with) the existing `## E2E harness contract`.
- **Phase 2 — Dev-tooling implementation.** `li-e2ecdt`: implement the harness (driver, discovery, coverage gate, step orchestrator) in `livespec-dev-tooling`. Cut a release. Self-tested in isolation against a tiny single-skill fixture-plugin.
- **Phase 3 — `livespec` consumer.** `li-e2eclv`: bump-pin dev-tooling; add `tests/e2e-cli/` fixtures for every `/livespec:*` skill; add CI job parametrized over known impl plugins.
- **Phase 4 — `livespec-impl-plaintext` consumer.** `li-e2ecpx`: bump-pin dev-tooling; add `tests/e2e-cli/` fixtures for every `/livespec-impl-plaintext:*` skill; wire CI; **first run will fail** by surfacing the two P1 bugs already filed, which then MUST be fixed before this sub-task closes.

## Side-effect closures (cross-repo)

Two existing impl-plaintext work-items are expected to be closed as a direct side effect of making the new harness pass in `livespec-impl-plaintext`:

- `li-k5dadf` (impl-plaintext) — Plugin bundle ships without `_vendor/livespec_runtime/`. Every cross-repo skill currently fails with `ModuleNotFoundError` from a fresh cache install. Phase 4 fixture invocations cannot succeed until this is fixed.
- `li-m4q4h5` (impl-plaintext) — Every SKILL.md quotes wrapper paths in the `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/...` source-repo form that does not survive Claude Code's installer flattening. Phase 4 fixture invocations cannot succeed until the documented invocation paths match the post-install layout.

These two records are not local dependencies of this epic (they live in a different repo's work-items store) but are explicitly tracked here so that closure of `li-e2ecpx` requires verifying both bugs are independently closed in impl-plaintext, with audit pointers cross-linking the resolution back to this epic.

## Ordering nuance

Phase 1 (spec contract) and Phase 2 (dev-tooling implementation) want to land roughly together because the contract describes the harness shape and the harness implementation references the contract. Suggested sequence: file the Phase 1 propose-change; in parallel begin Phase 2 implementation. Land Phase 1 via revise first (so the contract is committed); then release Phase 2; then Phase 3 and Phase 4 can proceed in parallel.

## Wontfix criteria

Human approval required before any sub-task may be closed; sub-agent work halts at PR open per `feedback-cross-repo-propose-change-must-merge` discipline.
reason: Epic complete: Phase 1 li-e2ecspec (#358 v097), Phase 2 li-e2ecdt (#73 v0.8.0), Phase 3 li-e2eclv (#362), Phase 4 li-e2ecpx (impl-plaintext #63) all merged. Top-of-pyramid CLI-driven user-surface e2e tier shipped across the plugin family.
title: Top-of-pyramid CLI-driven e2e harness for the livespec plugin family

description:
## Scope

Propose-change against `livespec/SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" adding a sub-rule that strengthens the existing heading-coverage invariant for `scenarios.md` entries specifically: each scenario's mapped test MUST live at the **middle (integration) or top (e2e / CLI) tier** of the test pyramid, not at the unit tier.

## Current state

`constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" (lines 219-235) mandates that every `##` heading in a template-declared NLSpec file have an entry in `tests/heading-coverage.json` mapping to a pytest node id. The rule is uniform across spec files — a unit test at `tests/livespec/commands/test_seed.py::...` satisfies the gate for a `## Scenario: ...` heading just as it does for a `## Specification model` heading.

But scenarios are categorically different: scenarios.md describes user-observable behavior. A scenario covered only by a unit test isn't really covered — the unit test verifies internal shape, not the user-observable round trip the scenario describes. Today's gate accepts that substitution silently.

## Proposed amendment

Add a sub-rule to §"Heading taxonomy" along these lines:

> For entries whose `spec_file` is `scenarios.md`, the mapped `test` node id MUST > resolve to a test in the integration tier or above. Operationally: the node-id > path component MUST begin with one of the integration-tier prefixes (e.g., > `tests.e2e.…`, `tests.integration.…`, `tests.prompts.…`, …) enumerated in the > per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist; OR the resolved > test function MUST carry an explicit `pytest.mark.integration` (or stronger) > marker. `TODO` entries are still allowed during transition but MUST carry a > reason explicitly acknowledging the tier requirement.

The check `livespec_dev_tooling.checks.heading_coverage` MUST be extended to enforce this sub-rule, with a new diagnostic class (e.g., `scenario heading mapped to unit-tier test`).

## Why a per-repo allowlist

Different repos place integration tests in different directories (`tests/e2e/`, `tests/integration/`, `tests/consumer/`, `tests/prompts/`). The rule should be structural (a prefix list) rather than hard-coded so each repo can declare its own convention without amending the shared check.

## Out of scope

- Re-tier-ing any existing `scenarios.md` entries that currently point at unit-tier tests. That's per-repo cleanup work (see Phases 2-3 of this epic for the library repos; livespec itself is already mostly-compliant per the audit in the epic body).
- Forcing the same rule on `contracts.md`, `constraints.md`, or `spec.md` headings. Those legitimately may be exercised by unit-tier tests; only scenarios are user-observable-by-definition.

## Acceptance

- New sub-rule accepted into `constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy" via propose-change → revise.
- `heading_coverage` check extended to enforce the scenario-tier rule, with the per-repo `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling.scenario_tiers]` allowlist mechanism.
- Dev-tooling release cut at the matching version; downstream repos can bump-pin to pick up the strengthened check.
- livespec's own `scenarios.md` entries audited: any currently pointing at unit-tier tests are re-pointed at e2e equivalents or moved to TODO with a tier-rationale reason.
reason: Re-opened: false-closed in PR #364. Only acceptance criterion 1/4 landed (constraints.md §Heading taxonomy scenarios.md tier sub-rule via PR #360 / revise v098). Criteria 2-4 — heading_coverage CHECK EXTENSION, dev-tooling Phase-1 release, livespec scenario audit — remain; in flight as epic li-scetier Phase 1 (Waves 1-2).
title: Strengthen livespec heading-coverage to require middle/top-of-pyramid tests for scenarios.md (epic li-scetier Phase 1)
